requirement: The TCS shall be capable of presenting to the operator a visual depiction of the minimum and maximum data link operational ranges.
ASR_Explanation: Line 2: This is a UI‐level functional detail that can be fulfilled with existing visualization components and does not drive core architectural decisions.

requirement: The TCS shall have the functionality to process payload product data from Electro Optical (EO), Infrared (IR), and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) payloads. This functionality, as a minimum, shall include: formatting, storing, internally routing, and recording the video; creating and storing a freeze frame of the video; retrieving and displaying the video; printing a hard copy of freeze frame video; and processing digital imagery for export and dissemination.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the core architecture for high-throughput multi-sensor data ingestion, processing, storage, and retrieval pipelines, impacting scalability and integration.

requirement: The TCS shall be able to store up to 24 hours of payload data. External storage can be utilized for this purpose.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives the design and sizing of the persistence layer and external storage integration, impacting data architecture and throughput.

requirement: The TCS shall be in compliance with Common Imagery Ground Surface Station (CIGSS), United States Imagery Standards (USIS), Video Working Group Standards Architecture, National Imagery Transmission Format (NITF) Version 2.0, and Global Command Control Systems (GCCS) when processing payload imagery data.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR imposes strict data format, interface, and compliance constraints on the imagery processing pipeline, directly shaping the system’s architecture.

requirement: The NITF 2.0 imagery files generated by the TCS shall contain the necessary telemetry and support data to permit subsequent imagery exploitation by C4I systems.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement dictates data format and metadata content critical to system interoperability and integration architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall have a built-in text entry capability including the ability to annotate textual information on imagery.
ASR_Explanation: This is a localized UI feature (text entry/annotation) that can be implemented within a standard presentation layer without altering the system’s core architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall be capable of receiving secondary HAE UAV payload imagery.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement impacts the system’s data ingestion architecture, network bandwidth allocation, and interface design for UAV imagery handling.

requirement: Payload telemetry data shall be available to support other TCS functions as required.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the system’s data distribution and integration architecture by requiring real-time telemetry interfaces and middleware support.

requirement: The TCS shall display live and recorded imagery data, with as well as without annotation and overlay, upon operator request.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design of the streaming, storage‐retrieval, and rendering pipeline (including annotation/overlay layering) and its performance characteristics.

requirement: The TCS shall provide the capability to simultaneously view imagery as well as data from more than one payload, when applicable.
ASR_Explanation: It requires architecture-level support for concurrent payload data streaming and integrated multi-source display capabilities.

requirement: The TCS operator shall be able to select the content of the overlay information.
ASR_Explanation: This is a UI‐level feature for overlay customization and does not drive significant changes to the system’s architectural structure.

requirement: The TCS shall have the capability to select and deselect several types of cross hairs (or other similar ICON) to identify a selected point on a target.
ASR_Explanation: This is a UI-level feature for crosshair selection with minimal impact on overall system architecture.

requirement: RS170A video and digital imagery shall be routed to TCS functions and displayed upon operator request.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement impacts the system’s data flow and streaming architecture by mandating real-time routing, storage, and display of RS170A video and imagery.

requirement: The TCS shall have the functionality to conduct limited exploitation, to include voice and textual reporting for spot and mission objectives, on the payload product data. Limited exploitation, as a minimum, will include image enhancement and annotation.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design of new payload data processing and reporting subsystems (image enhancement, annotation, voice/text reporting).

requirement: The image enhancement capability shall include contrast, brightness, edge enhancement, and sharpness.
ASR_Explanation: This is a detailed functional requirement that does not impose structural or quality constraints driving the overall system architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall provide the capability to capture frozen-frames of imagery and store these frozen images for further review and processing.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the design of a high-throughput image capture and storage subsystem, impacting dataflow, storage capacity, and performance architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall have the capability to display Near-Real Time (NRT) imagery to include, as a minimum, date/time group, target location coordinates when the target is in the center of the field of view, north seeking arrow, and AV position and heading.
ASR_Explanation: This drives the real‐time data ingestion, sensor integration, processing pipeline, and UI rendering architecture for NRT imagery and overlayed metadata.

requirement: The TCS shall provide the capability to compute the range and bearing between two geographic positions located on the payload imagery display.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard functional calculation requirement that does not impose major architectural constraints.

requirement: The TCS shall support a target location function where the operator can request the current ground location of the payload field-of-view center.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement mandates integration of real-time geolocation processing and coordinate-transformation services, affecting system modules and external interfaces.

requirement: The TCS shall have the functionality to determine the location of items of interest within the payload field of view, and express these locations in coordinates acceptable for military applications.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design of the sensor-processing pipeline, coordinate-transformation modules, and external mapping interfaces.

requirement: The TCS shall have the functionality to develop an estimate of the error in computed target coordinates, and associate the error estimate with the appropriate target.
ASR_Explanation: This drives the need for a dedicated error‐estimation component and impacts data models and interfaces for propagating coordinate uncertainties.

requirement: The TCS shall be capable of entering DII-COE compliant (C4I) networks.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement dictates the system’s network and security architecture to support DII-COE compliant C4I connectivity.

requirement: The TCS shall have the functionality necessary to manage all aspects of C4I system interfaces to include receiving, processing, and transmitting tactical information to include but not limited to character based text messages, NITF 2.0 imagery files, and RS-170A video.
ASR_Explanation: It drives core architectural decisions for interface layering, data processing modules, and communication protocols to support varied tactical data formats.

requirement: The TCS shall provide the functionality necessary to interface with various C4I systems in order to satisfy the operational requirements for: Tasking TCS to plan and conduct a mission; Presentation of payload product and target coordinates for export and dissemination; Use of UAV obtained data (Non-real time tracks, tactical points and amplifying information) to provide a C4I system with information that may be used by C4I system operators, for transmission on tactical data communication links, and available to support engagement by appropriate weapons systems.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the system’s architectural design for standardized interfaces, data exchange protocols, and integration services to ensure C4I interoperability.

requirement: The TCS shall have the capability to interoperate with a data server to receive, extract, and push intelligence data.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the integration architecture by defining communication protocols, data transformation layers, and middleware components for server interoperability.

requirement: The TCS shall have the capability to use cable to deliver live video imagery in multiple locations.
ASR_Explanation: This cable-based live video delivery requirement drives the network/infrastructure architecture, affecting topology, bandwidth provisioning, and component interfaces.

requirement: The TCS shall have the ability to interface with Service specific ground and airborne Ultra High Frequency (UHF), Very High Frequency (VHF), UHF/VHF, and High Frequency (HF) radios for digital message transmission while using the same radios for record traffic.
ASR_Explanation: It dictates specialized hardware interface layers and communication protocols for concurrent digital messaging over multiple radio bands.

requirement: Where applicable, TCS data burst messages shall comply with Variable Message Formats.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement dictates the messaging protocol and data serialization layer, impacting the design of the system’s communication architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall export and disseminate formatted NITF 2.0 files, digital imagery (if applicable), RS-170A video (with as well as without overlay), and tactical communication messages.
ASR_Explanation: This drives the design of multi-format encoding/streaming pipelines, interface protocols, and high-throughput dissemination infrastructure.

requirement: The TCS will have the capability to manage all operational C4I interfaces across all 5 levels of interaction.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the system’s core integration architecture by mandating support for all C4I interfaces across five interaction levels, affecting modularity and communication patterns.

requirement: The TCS shall, as a minimum, have the functionality to provide the following control capabilities: Send and receive tactical communication messages; Send and receive annotated and un-annotated digital imagery; Establish digital communication and when completed terminate communications to the C4I systems specified in Section 3.2.5; Establish and when completed terminate digital communication to peripheral devices.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the definition of communication interfaces, protocols, security layers, and lifecycle management in the system architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall, as a minimum, have the functionality to provide the following control capabilities: Send and receive analog imagery in RS-170A format with as well as without overlay; Establish and when completed terminate analog communication to C4I systems specified in Section 3.2.5; Establish and when completed terminate analog communication to peripheral devices.
ASR_Explanation: This drives the design of hardware interface modules and protocol layers for analog RS-170A communication, impacting system decomposition and integration.

requirement: The TCS will have the functionality to process received C4I messages and to prepare C4I messages for transmission. Processing will include formatting, storing, routing, and display.
ASR_Explanation: This core messaging functionality will drive the system’s component architecture, data storage/routing layers, and user interface design.

requirement: The TCS shall, as a minimum, have the functionality to provide the following digital data processing capabilities: Create Tactical Communications Messages to include United States Message Text Format (USMTF), Tactical Fire (TACFIRE), Over The Horizon Gold (OTH-Gold), and Intelligence and Electronic Warfare Character Oriented Message Catalog (IEWCOMCAT) For Transmission; Review Incoming Tactical Communication Messages; Prepare Annotated as well as Un-annotated Digital Imagery For Transmission; Review Incoming Annotated as well as Un-annotated Digital Imagery.
ASR_Explanation: This drives a modular, plug-in architecture for handling multiple tactical message formats and imagery annotation pipelines, heavily influencing system interfaces and data flow.

requirement: All digital messages received by the TCS shall be automatically checked for errors and corrected when possible.
ASR_Explanation: Integrating automatic error detection and correction into the communication layer impacts protocol design and core subsystem architecture.

requirement: All erroneous messages that cannot be corrected by TCS shall be flagged to the operator.
ASR_Explanation: It mandates a cross‐cutting error detection and operator notification mechanism, impacting the messaging, monitoring, and alerting architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall provide the capability to log all incoming and outgoing formatted tactical messages.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement impacts the system architecture by dictating the design of a scalable, high-throughput logging framework with storage, security, and audit capabilities.

requirement: The TCS shall, as a minimum, have the functionality to provide the following analog data processing capabilities: Prepare annotated as well as un-annotated analog imagery for transmission; Receive incoming annotated as well as un-annotated analog imagery.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design of the analog imagery processing and transmission subsystems, including metadata handling and interface requirements.

requirement: The TCS shall have the functionality to monitor the status of all C4I interfaces, and display appropriate control information.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement impacts the system’s interface integration, real-time monitoring mechanisms, and UI architecture for status display.

requirement: The TCS shall, as a minimum, have the functionality to provide the following data monitoring capabilities: Display which C4I systems are supported and online during a mission; Monitor the status of all incoming and outgoing tactical communication messages; Review all tactical communication messages received and transmitted; View incoming and outgoing, annotated as well as un-annotated analog imagery; View incoming and outgoing, annotated as well as un-annotated digital imagery messages.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the messaging integration, real‐time monitoring, data storage/processing, and UI subsystems, impacting the overall system architecture.

requirement: TCS shall be capable of executing AV maintenance software and displaying appropriate status results.
ASR_Explanation: It mandates a modular execution environment and UI subsystem to host AV maintenance software and display its status.

requirement: TCS shall be capable of executing payload maintenance software and displaying appropriate status results.
ASR_Explanation: This is a routine functional capability with minimal impact on overall system structure or quality attributes.

requirement: TCS shall be capable of executing data terminal maintenance software and displaying appropriate status results.
ASR_Explanation: This is a straightforward functional capability (running maintenance software and showing status) with minimal impact on overall system architecture.

requirement: TCS shall be capable of executing workstation and peripheral equipment maintenance software and displaying appropriate status results.
ASR_Explanation: This is a routine functional requirement for maintenance capabilities that doesn’t impose major architectural constraints.

requirement: Fault Detection/Location (FD/L) to the Line Replaceable Unit (LRU) level shall be provided to indicate the readiness status of TCS, if inherent to the COTS hardware and software utilized.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the diagnostic and monitoring architecture by requiring LRU-level fault detection and readiness reporting within the COTS hardware/software integration.

requirement: As a minimum, TCS shall provide Startup FD/L for the operator workstation.
ASR_Explanation: This is a detailed operator‐workstation startup display requirement that doesn’t drive core system architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall allow the operator to control and monitor the AV's FD/L, if required and provided by the Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM); Payload's FD/L, if required and provided by the Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM); and Data Link FD/L, if required and provided by the Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM).
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives a modular, pluggable interface architecture to integrate and manage optional OEM-provided FD/L subsystems.

requirement: The TCS shall allow authorized operators to install software upgrades via CD-ROM as well as other media storage devices.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the system’s update architecture by requiring modular storage-interface support and secure operator authorization.

requirement: The TCS shall restrict operator access to this capability via password protection.
ASR_Explanation: It introduces a security/authentication component affecting the system’s modular design and credential management architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall provide the capability for Authorized Operators to modify all TCS programmable parameters. As a minimum, the TCS shall restrict Operator access to this capability via password protection.
ASR_Explanation: Introducing password‐protected access for parameter modification mandates designing and integrating an authentication/authorization subsystem at the architectural level.

requirement: The TCS shall be capable of importing National Imagery Mapping Agency (NIMA) Digital Terrain Elevation Data (DTED), Digital Feature Analysis Data (DFAD), Arc Digitized Raster Graphic and scanned hard copy maps, via compact disk.
ASR_Explanation: This mandates integration of multiple geospatial data import pipelines with CD-based ingestion, impacting system interfaces, parsing modules, and storage architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall be capable of importing map information via operator procedure and shall be capable of incorporating vector format and Compressed ADRG (CADRG) maps.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement affects the system’s data ingestion and processing architecture by mandating modules and interfaces for vector and CADRG map format support.

requirement: The TCS shall allow an Authorized Operator to execute a software debug capability and view the resulting debug diagnostic information. As a minimum, the TCS will restrict Operator access to this capability via password protection.
ASR_Explanation: It requires integrating a password‐protected debug interface, impacting the system’s security and modular access control architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall provide the capability to interface with equipment necessary to provide connectivity with standard DoD tactical (VHF, UHF, and UHF/VHF) radios, Mobile Subscriber Equipment, and military and commercial satellite communications equipment.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the overall system interface architecture by requiring specific hardware/software modules, protocols, and security measures to integrate with DoD radios and satellite equipment.

requirement: The TCS shall interface with external mission tasking systems (e.g., receive tasking orders, coordinate mission certification).
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design of external integration interfaces, communication protocols, middleware components, and message handling architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall provide the system functionality necessary to interface with the data terminal.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard functional interface requirement that doesn’t impose significant high-level architectural constraints.

requirement: The TCS shall provide the system functionality to allow interfacing with external systems via a local area network.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the system’s network interface architecture, impacting protocols, security, and integration layers.

requirement: The TCS shall provide external interfaces to launch and recovery systems.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement defines critical external interfaces that will shape the system’s integration architecture, module boundaries, and associated security and performance constraints.

requirement: The TCS shall implement an AV Standard Interface that will provide the proper data format to ensure communications with the selected AV.
ASR_Explanation: Specifying the AV Standard Interface and data format drives component boundaries, integration protocols, and overall system architecture.

requirement: This interface shall allow for addition of future AVs and will provide the generic architecture to ensure interoperability.
ASR_Explanation: This mandates a modular, extensible interface and interoperability framework, shaping the system’s core architecture for integrating future AVs.

requirement: The uplink and downlink information passed between the TCS and the AV shall be in accordance with the associated AV documentation.
ASR_Explanation: This is an implementation‐level interface compliance requirement that doesn’t drive high‐level architectural structure.

requirement: The TCS shall support a concurrent uplink and downlink capability.
ASR_Explanation: It requires a full-duplex communication design affecting the system’s concurrency model, threading, and network interface architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall provide an internal interface for establishing communications with the Real Time Processor (RTP) within TCS.
ASR_Explanation: Defines a critical internal interface with the Real Time Processor, impacting module boundaries, communication protocols, and real-time architecture.

requirement: This interface shall allow the information from the data server to be made available to other components of the TCS.
ASR_Explanation: It defines a key integration interface for data flow, impacting component boundaries and communication architecture.

requirement: The RTP interface shall support distributed processing capability.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement impacts component decomposition, communication protocols, and deployment topology by mandating distributed RTP processing.

requirement: Remotely hosted applications shall communicate in a client server relationship via the defined data server interface.
ASR_Explanation: It enforces a client–server communication pattern and defined interface, shaping system topology, component distribution, and integration.

requirement: The TCS shall provide an internal interface for the SAR Processor in order to disseminate SAR information (to include imagery and telemetry) to other components of the TCS.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement defines critical subsystem boundaries and communication interfaces that will shape the overall system architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall incorporate an intercom system that provides verbal communicate in the situation where there are multiple operators.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design and integration of an audio communication subsystem and impacts system interfaces, networking, and infrastructure.

requirement: The intercom system shall be compatible with service specific voice communication systems.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the system integration architecture by requiring support for multiple voice‐communication protocols and adapter layers.

requirement: The TCS shall allow the Operator(s) to fully control the VCR via the TCS Display input device(s).
ASR_Explanation: This is a detailed UI‐level integration requirement that doesn’t drive major architectural decisions.

requirement: The TCS shall be able to route VCR recorded payload video to the C4I Interfaces.
ASR_Explanation: It defines critical data flow and interface requirements that will impact network architecture, integration points, and real-time throughput handling.

requirement: An RS-170A video interface shall be provided for the system to output and input analog imagery and overlays to and from a Video Cassette Recorder (VCR) for recording and playback.
ASR_Explanation: It mandates inclusion of an RS-170A analog video I/O subsystem, impacting hardware interface design and driver architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall have ports for outputting data and imagery to a n internal hard copy printer.
ASR_Explanation: This is a low-level hardware interface detail for printer connectivity and does not drive high-level architectural decisions.

requirement: The TCS shall, as a minimum, allow the Operator(s) to print freeze-frame video, C4I Messages, Mission Plans, FD/L information, and current map display to an internal printer.
ASR_Explanation: It introduces a cross‐cutting printing service requiring integration with hardware drivers and data‐formatting pipelines across multiple subsystems.

requirement: The TCS shall be able to access data storage devices.
ASR_Explanation: This generic data access requirement can be handled by standard persistence layers and doesn’t drive any specific architectural pattern or constraint.

requirement: The TCS shall have the functionality to transfer digital data as well as digital imagery to and from data storage devices.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard functional requirement for data handling that does not impose significant constraints on the high-level architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall provide a CD drive for the retrieval of TCS data.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement imposes a hardware interface constraint, impacting system integration, driver support, and data retrieval architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall provide a tape drive for storage and retrieval of TCS data.
ASR_Explanation: Mandates tape-based storage integration, impacting hardware selection, I/O interfaces, and data retrieval design.

requirement: The TCS shall provide a RAID for storage and retrieval of TCS data, if required.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement impacts the storage subsystem architecture by mandating RAID for data redundancy, performance, and availability.

requirement: The TCS shall have an interface to an uninterruptible power supply.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement impacts the hardware–software integration and power management architecture, affecting system reliability and availability.

requirement: The TCS design shall consider all safety requirements affecting design and performance except nuclear safety.
ASR_Explanation: It imposes critical safety constraints that will drive architectural decisions on component isolation, redundancy, and fault-tolerance.

requirement: The TCS safety requirements are intended to eliminate as well as control potential hazards to equipment and personnel involved in the TCS. The TCS shall comply with para 5.3 of MIL-STD 882C, “System Safety Program Requirements”, dated 19 January 1993 w/ Notice 1 dated 19 January 1996.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR mandates integration of MIL-STD 882C safety controls and hazard mitigation measures into the system architecture, affecting module design and interfaces.

requirement: The TCS shall provide sufficient cues to allow the operator to safely take-off, land and navigate under Instrument Flight Rules.
ASR_Explanation: This safety-critical IFR cueing requirement drives the real-time data processing, sensor integration, and display subsystems in the system architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall provide adequate capability to allow the operator to operate each UAV within its certified operational flight envelope.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the real-time control, safety-critical interfaces, and communications architecture for UAV operations.

requirement: Appropriate cautions and warnings shall be provided to the operator if the air vehicle deviates into unsafe flight regime.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design of real-time monitoring and alerting subsystems, impacting module partitioning, performance budgets, and safety fault handling.

requirement: For mission planning, the TCS shall provide terrain avoidance warning and minimum reception altitude calculations for line of sight flights.
ASR_Explanation: It mandates real-time terrain data integration, safety-critical warning modules, and altitude computation services, shaping system architecture, data flows, and performance requirements.

requirement: During mission execution, the TCS shall provide the operator a cautions and warnings when the UAV system has identified a malfunction.
ASR_Explanation: This is a straightforward functional requirement for error notification without imposing structural or quality-attribute constraints.

requirement: The TCS shall provide the required information to allow the operator to maintain safe separation from other aircraft and a safe altitude in civilian airspace per Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) rules.
ASR_Explanation: Line 2: It imposes safety-critical, real-time and regulatory compliance constraints that drive the system’s high-integrity, fault-tolerant architecture and sensor integration.

requirement: The TCS shall be designed such that no single hardware failure results in an unsafe command to be transmitted to the air vehicle.
ASR_Explanation: This safety requirement drives the system’s fault-tolerant, redundant hardware architecture to prevent single-point failures.

requirement: The TCS shall be designed such that no single software error results in an unsafe command to be transmitted to the air vehicle.
ASR_Explanation: This safety requirement drives the need for fault‐tolerant, redundant error detection and mitigation mechanisms in the system architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall be capable of restoring power in sufficient time to avoid loss of air vehicle control during power outages.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the architecture of redundant/backup power subsystems and fast-switching mechanisms to meet stringent recovery time requirements.

requirement: The TCS shall monitor the uplink and downlink to each UAV under its control.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the design of the communications subsystem to support real-time monitoring, reliability, and bandwidth management for UAV uplink/downlink.

requirement: Upon detection of loss of link, the TCS shall attempt to reestablish communications with the air vehicle.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design of the communications subsystem’s fault-detection and reconnection strategies, affecting subsystem partitioning and reliability architecture.

requirement: The TCS design shall provide protection against injury to TCS operators and maintenance personnel.
ASR_Explanation: This safety requirement drives inclusion of fail-safe mechanisms, hazard analysis, and isolation layers in the system architecture.

requirement: System safety and health hazards, if any, shall be identified and evaluated during Phase I of the TCS development.
ASR_Explanation: It mandates early safety analysis and hazard mitigation, shaping system decomposition and safety-critical module design.

requirement: Risk levels and a program to manage the probability and severity of hazards shall also be developed.
ASR_Explanation: It mandates a dedicated risk‐management subsystem with specific data flows and interfaces, impacting component design and system integration.

requirement: The TCS is an Automated Information System (AIS). Therefore, as per DoD Regulation 5000.2-R, dated March 15, 1996, the TCS shall meet security requirements in accordance with DoD Directive 5200.28(D), “Security Requirements for Automated Information Systems” dated March 21, 1988.
ASR_Explanation: This DoD-mandated security requirement dictates core security architecture, controls, and compliance frameworks.

requirement: The TCS shall be accredited by the Designated Approving Authority prior to processing classified as well as sensitive unclassified data.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement imposes security accreditation and compliance constraints that will drive the system’s security architecture and trust boundary design.

requirement: Using risk assessment procedures defined in DoD 5200.28(D), a risk index and the minimum security requirements for TCS shall be determined.
ASR_Explanation: This security requirement drives the system’s high-level architecture by mandating integration of DoD risk assessment processes and enforcement of security controls.

requirement: The TCS data sensitivities shall be determined by the data sensitivities of the systems with which it interfaces, to including the air vehicles, payloads, and C4I systems.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the security and data-handling architecture by enforcing consistent classification, encryption, and access controls across system interfaces.

requirement: Links that provide communications between the TCS and other systems shall be secured in a manner appropriate for the sensitivities of the material passed through such links, in accordance with DoD Directive C-5200.5, “Communication Security (COMSEC)” dated 21 April 1990.
ASR_Explanation: This security requirement impacts the architecture by mandating secure communication channels, encryption, and key management per DoD COMSEC standards.

requirement: The TCS shall be designed to protect its communication and data links against enemy Electronic Warfare (EW) threats, physical anti-radiation weaponry and physical destruction.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the need for hardened, redundant communication architectures with anti‐jam, shielding, encryption and physical protection measures.

requirement: All hardware, software, documentation, and sensitive information processed by TCS shall be physically protected, minimally at the level determined by the risk index computed in Section 3.8.1, to prevent intentional as well as unintentional disclosure, destruction, and modification.
ASR_Explanation: This security NFR drives infrastructure and deployment architecture by requiring risk‐based physical security controls and zoning for all system components.

requirement: The TCS shall be approved for operation at the same level as the systems with which it interfaces.
ASR_Explanation: It mandates compliance and interoperability standards at interface points, impacting component design, integration, and certification architecture.

requirement: All TCS users, operators, maintainers and other personnel having access to TCS shall be cleared to the highest sensitivity of the data that the TCS processes, stores and transfers.
ASR_Explanation: This is an administrative security clearance policy with no direct impact on the system’s structural design.

requirement: Additional local site procedures shall be developed to prevent the intentional or unintentional disclosure of sensitive information to unauthorized individuals.
ASR_Explanation: This is a policy-level security procedure and does not impose direct technical or structural constraints on the system architecture.

requirement: A training program consisting of an initial security training and awareness briefing covering AIS security in general but also tailored to the TCS shall be developed.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement pertains to procedural training and awareness and does not impose constraints or drive decisions in the system’s architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall be capable of operation within environments specified in the System/Subsystem Design Document for the land-based shelter and shipboard environments.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR imposes environmental constraints that will drive hardware selection, enclosure design, and system reliability architecture.

requirement: The TCS hardware shall be mounted as well as ruggedized to withstand inter and intra theater movement.
ASR_Explanation: It imposes physical design and packaging constraints affecting hardware selection and overall system integration.

requirement: The TCS hardware will provide the functionality and capability to receive, process, and disseminate video and telemetry data from the AV and payload; perform mission planning; monitor and control the payload; monitor and control the AV; and monitor and control the data links.
ASR_Explanation: It defines critical data flows and control functions that will shape system partitioning, interfaces, and performance requirements.

requirement: The hardware of the TCS shall be capable of being scaled as well as being modular to meet the varying needs of the Services.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives hardware infrastructure and modular system architecture decisions to ensure scalable provisioning across services.

requirement: The TCS hardware shall allow for long range communications from one TCS to another.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement dictates the communication hardware and network architecture (radio modules, antennas, protocols, and range planning) for TCS-to-TCS links.

requirement: The TCS hardware shall allow for data storage expansion.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives hardware modularity and scalability, impacting system architecture and capacity planning.

requirement: The TCS hardware shall allow access to other computers to share in processing capability.
ASR_Explanation: It dictates a distributed architecture requiring network communication, concurrency management, and resource-sharing mechanisms.

requirement: The TCS hardware shall allow for multiple external peripherals.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement impacts the system’s hardware bus architecture and scalability planning for peripheral interface support.

requirement: The TCS hardware shall support the data rate characteristics of the AV, data link and payload to ensure interoperability.
ASR_Explanation: It imposes critical throughput and interface design constraints on hardware and network architecture to ensure interoperability.

requirement: For each OUTRIDER system, the TCS shall provide full independent computer redundancy.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives the system’s hardware and software architecture by requiring independent redundant compute units and failover mechanisms.

requirement: The TCS shall conform with the National Institute for Standard Technology (NIST) Federal Information Processing Standard (FIPS) Publication 151-2 (POSIX.1).
ASR_Explanation: This drives key architectural decisions around platform/OS selection and POSIX compliance layers to meet NIST FIPS 151-2.

requirement: The TCS shall have sufficient throughput to support the processing requirements of the selected data link.
ASR_Explanation: This throughput requirement drives performance and scalability decisions, influencing component design, data flow, and resource allocation.

requirement: To meet growth requirements, the TCS shall be capable of adding additional storage without major hardware reconfiguration.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the system’s scalability and infrastructure architecture, requiring a modular storage design that supports dynamic expansion without downtime.

requirement: The TCS computer system shall contain a CD-ROM drive that is compatible with National Imagery and Mapping Agency (NIMA), CD-ROM Digital Terrain Elevation Data (DTED), Digital Feature Analysis Data (DFAD), and embedded training media.
ASR_Explanation: It imposes specific hardware and driver compatibility constraints, affecting the I/O subsystem architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall use standard military worldwide 110/220 volt 50/60 hertz generators and commercial power sources.
ASR_Explanation: This constraint drives the system’s power architecture, requiring hardware design for dual-voltage/frequency input and appropriate power conversion.

requirement: The TCS shall use standard electrical power sources available within the DoD family of ground mobile, airborne, and shipboard electrical power sources.
ASR_Explanation: It imposes constraints on the hardware power subsystem requiring support for multiple DoD standard voltage and frequency inputs, impacting power management and component selection.

requirement: The TCS shall be capable of restoring power in sufficient time to avoid loss of critical mission data and loss of air vehicle control during power outages.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives the design of redundant power infrastructure and failover mechanisms to ensure system availability and resilience.

requirement: The TCS shall have an uninterrupted power supply for critical phases (landing and takeoff as a minimum) of mission execution.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives the overall system architecture to include redundant/backup power subsystems and high-availability power management for critical mission phases.

requirement: The TCS throughput shall not exceed 50% of the throughput capability delivered over any 10 second period, and as an objective throughput shall not exceed 25% of throughput capability delivered over any 10 second period.
ASR_Explanation: It imposes strict throughput limits requiring architectural provisions for capacity planning, rate-limiting, and resource management.

requirement: The TCS shall be capable of providing a 50% spare memory storage capacity over delivered storage used. As an objective a 75% spare memory storage capacity over storage used shall be provided.
ASR_Explanation: This capacity overprovisioning requirement drives storage subsystem sizing, resource allocation strategy, and scalability architecture decisions.

requirement: The TCS software will provide the functionality and capability to receive, process, and disseminate video and telemetry data from the AV and payload; perform mission planning; monitor and control the payload; monitor and control the AV; and monitor and control the data links.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the high-level decomposition into core subsystems (data processing, mission planning, control, and communications) and defines their interfaces.

requirement: The TCS shall have software based on Defense Information Infrastructure/Common Operating Environment per Assistant Secretary of Defense for Command, Control, Communications, and Intelligence (ASD(C3I)) Joint Technical Architecture (JTA).
ASR_Explanation: This NFR imposes compliance with the DII/COE architecture, constraining platform/OS choice, middleware, and interoperability layers.

requirement: The TCS shall comply with the Assistant Secretary of Defense (C3I) Joint Technical Architecture (JTA). This includes, but is not limited to, the language, the computer, database, architecture, and interoperability.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR imposes mandatory architectural constraints and technology standards (language, database, interoperability) per the DoD JTA.

requirement: The TCS shall provide an open software architecture to be capable of supporting additional CSCIs, CSCs, and CSUs for future AVs, future payloads, and payload capabilities (e.g. auto-search and automatic target tracking), and future Tactical UAVs.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the system’s high-level modularity and extensibility, mandating an open architecture to accommodate future components.

requirement: The TCS core software shall be generically written to provide level one through level five interaction for both Outrider and Predator UAVs and establish the architecture for future tactical UAVs.
ASR_Explanation: This drives a generic, extensible multi-platform architecture to support levels 1–5 interactions for current and future UAVs.

requirement: The TCS software shall provide the UAV operator with the necessary tools for computer related communications, mission tasking, mission planning, mission execution, data receipt, data processing, and data dissemination.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement defines core system capabilities and modules (communications, planning, execution, data pipeline) that drive high-level architectural decomposition and integration.

requirement: The TCS software shall be capable of being hosted on a variety of computer operating systems that are organic to the various Services.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design of platform abstraction layers and influences technology and deployment architecture for OS portability.

requirement: TCS software shall provide a windows based graphic operator interface.
ASR_Explanation: Specifying a Windows-based graphical operator interface constrains the platform and UI framework choices, impacting overall system architecture.

requirement: The TCS software shall be non-proprietary and have unlimited data rights.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR impacts architecture by dictating open‐source/commercial component selection, licensing compliance, and data management strategies.

requirement: The TCS’s operating system and executable software shall be re-programmable without hardware disassembly.
ASR_Explanation: This drives the architecture of the upgrade mechanism—requiring a modular bootloader, secure in-field update interface, and hardware abstraction for runtime reprogramming.

requirement: Training software shall be alterable without affecting the configuration of the operational software.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives a modular architecture with isolated training and operational components to ensure independent configuration and updates.

requirement: The TCS software shall restrict the operator(s) from exercising levels of interaction not achievable by the system.
ASR_Explanation: This is a routine functional constraint on user input/interaction with minimal impact on overall system architecture.

requirement: The TCS software shall provide a high resolution, computer generated, graphical user interface that enables the UAV operator that is trained on one system to control different types of UAVs as well as UAV payloads with minimal additional training.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the choice of a modular, high-performance UI framework with abstraction layers to support multiple UAV types and payloads.

requirement: Each control console shall have, at a minimum, the capability to display the following four display windows: (1) display to provide aircraft position, TCS position, flight path, and a waypoint graphics in the foreground which are positioned in relation to a map displayed in the background, (2) display to provide aircraft flight data or payload data in the foreground, and downlinked video in the background. (3)A display to provide graphic presentations of downlinked telemetry data, and (4) display to present the interface menus for workstation software.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the design of the graphics/rendering and window‐management architecture to support multi‐layered, concurrent display requirements.

requirement: The DII/COE UCP/CMP shall provide a consistent and common set of interfaces for United States Message Text Format (USMTF), Army Tactical Command Control System (ATCSS), and Field Artillery Tactical Data Systems (FATDS) message sets.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design of core middleware interfaces and message translation adapters, affecting system modularity and interoperability architecture.

requirement: The TACCOM segment shall provide API’s for the transmission of imagery in National Imagery Transmission Formats 1.1a and 2.0 as per MILSTD-2500 and to be compatible with the Common Imagery Ground/Surface Station (CGIS) Guidelines.
ASR_Explanation: Mandating MIL-STD-2500/NITF formats and CGIS compatibility drives key decisions in the API/interface design and data-processing architecture.

requirement: The TCS data latency shall not be greater than that present in the Predator ground control station or Outrider ground control station, whichever is smaller.
ASR_Explanation: It imposes strict low-latency constraints that will drive choices in network topology, hardware, and real-time data processing architecture.

requirement: The TCS reliability will be considered in every phase of the design and development process and shall achieve a system reliability (Mean Time Between Failures MTBF) equal to or greater than that which is specified in the Predator and Outrider ORDs.
ASR_Explanation: This reliability NFR drives architecture-level decisions on redundancy, fault tolerance, and error-handling to meet the specified MTBF.

requirement: The TCS maintainability will be considered in every phase of the design and development process and shall achieve a system maintainability (Mean Time To Repair MTTR) equal to or greater than that which is specified in the Predator and Outrider ORDs.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives architectural decisions around modularity, service boundaries, and maintainability frameworks to meet specified MTTR targets.

requirement: The TCS equipment shall achieve an availability (A o), as defined below, equal to or greater than that which is specified in the Predator and Outrider ORDs.
ASR_Explanation: This availability requirement drives key architectural decisions around redundancy, fault-tolerance, and system resilience.

requirement: The total, fully useable, addressable, physically present program instruction memory and data storage memory for each processor shall have at least 50% unused memory during the Normal Operations Mode over any 10 second period.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives architectural decisions on processor memory sizing, capacity planning, and overprovisioning.

requirement: The processing speed of each processor shall be such that at least 50% of the throughput of each processor remains unused over all 10 second periods and at least 20% of the throughput of each processor remains unused over one second periods regardless of the system function performed.
ASR_Explanation: This throughput headroom requirement will drive processor sizing, resource allocation strategies, and overall system performance architecture.

requirement: The I/O channel reserve capability for each processor shall have at least a 50% reserve, addressable and useable, I/O channel capacity over any 10 second period.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the I/O subsystem architecture by mandating capacity reservation, scheduling policies, and hardware provisioning to guarantee 50% spare throughput.

requirement: TCS software flexibility and expandability shall be provided through use of the DII COE and through use of standardized software development practices.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives key architectural decisions by mandating the DII COE framework and standardized development practices, impacting modularity and extensibility.

requirement: Hardware and software shall be selected for use in the TCS with the goal of providing ease of future changes to the TCS elements.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives architectural decisions around modularity, extensibility, and technology selection to ensure system modifiability.

requirement: The selection of processors, interface cards for communication interfaces, disk drives, video, networking equipment, and all other hardware for use in the TCS shall be made according to standards for production of an open architecture.
ASR_Explanation: This mandates open hardware platform standards, directly impacting system modularity, interoperability, and vendor independence.

requirement: The selection of operating system and programming language for use in the TCS shall be made according to standards for development of an open architecture.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR imposes fundamental technology and platform choices that shape system interoperability, portability, and overall architecture.

requirement: Testability shall be considered in the design and development of the TCS.
ASR_Explanation: It drives architectural decisions on component modularity, interfaces, dependency injection, and hooks to support automated testing.

requirement: The system shall be functionally and physically partitioned to allow for efficient fault isolation.
ASR_Explanation: This drives the system’s modular decomposition and deployment topology to enforce fault isolation and resilience.

requirement: Control over internal items and devices shall be provided for detecting and isolating internal faults.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the inclusion of fault-detection and isolation components, impacting module boundaries, monitoring infrastructure, and overall system reliability.

requirement: Test points and data paths shall be defined to support efficient fault isolation.
ASR_Explanation: Defines required testability hooks and data-path instrumentation, impacting component interfaces, observability design, and overall system wiring.

requirement: The TCS shall provide the common software architecture for TCS interaction with Predator, Outrider, and future Tactical UAVs.
ASR_Explanation: Defines the core architectural framework for cross-platform UAV integrations, driving modularity and extensibility.

requirement: In the selection of hardware design solutions to satisfy the requirements herein, Non-Developmental Items (NDI) (off-the-shelf equipment previously approved for service use) shall be chosen to the maximum practicable extent.
ASR_Explanation: It constrains the hardware architecture by mandating maximal use of COTS/NDI components, affecting platform selection, integration and maintainability.

requirement: System documentation shall be developed as part of the TCS program and will follow MIL-STD-498 for format.
ASR_Explanation: This is a documentation format requirement (MIL-STD-498) that does not impact the system’s architecture.

requirement: The documentation developed shall contain sufficient level of detail to identify the functional, operational and design requirements of the TCS.
ASR_Explanation: This is a documentation quality requirement and does not impose constraints on architectural decisions.

requirement: The documentation shall contain sufficient technical detail to define the hardware and software design implemented to satisfy the system requirements.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement specifies documentation detail levels and does not impose constraints on the system’s architectural structure or key design decisions.

requirement: TCS material factors shall be governed by the NDI, GFE and COTS specifications developed by the equipment manufacturers, where applicable.
ASR_Explanation: It imposes external specification constraints on component materials and interfaces, affecting system design, integration, and compliance.

requirement: During Phase 1, control techniques to minimize electromagnetic interference, emanation, and susceptibility shall be used in the design of TCS equipment.
ASR_Explanation: This EMI/EMC constraint drives key architectural decisions on equipment layout, shielding, filtering, and component selection.

requirement: There shall be neither unacceptable response nor malfunction of any TCS and associated equipment due to EMI produced by any as well as all of the TCS and equipment associated with the TCS.
ASR_Explanation: This EMI immunity requirement drives hardware architecture decisions on shielding, filtering, and component placement to ensure reliable operation.

requirement: The TCS shall be compatible with the external electromagnetic environment that is typical of the service specific environment in the TCS will be operated.
ASR_Explanation: This electromagnetic compatibility NFR drives hardware architecture and enclosure design to ensure the system meets environmental interference requirements.

requirement: The TCS design shall ensure that personnel, fuel, and ordinance are not exposed to electromagnetic radiation as a result of operating the TCS.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the system’s physical architecture by imposing EMI‐shielding, materials selection, and spatial layout constraints.

requirement: Newly designed software shall be developed in accordance with a tailored MIL-STD-498.
ASR_Explanation: It imposes rigorous MIL-STD-498 compliance, driving architecture artifacts, design reviews, and configuration management processes.

requirement: Software written for other systems shall be used in TCS where it is determined that the existing software is suitable for use within the TCS software.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the integration architecture by introducing external dependencies and interface‐adaptation requirements for reused components.

requirement: A modular architecture shall be used by the TCS software in order to support future interoperability with multiple types of UAVs and payloads while maintaining consistent displays and user interfaces.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives the structuring of the system into loosely coupled, extensible modules with defined interfaces to ensure future UAV/payload interoperability and consistent UIs.

requirement: TCS hardware flexibility and expansion shall be provided through use of GFE, NDI and COTS hardware designed to be upgraded and expanded.
ASR_Explanation: It mandates a modular, extensible hardware architecture supporting GFE, NDI, and COTS components to enable future upgrades and expansion.

requirement: After emplacement at the operational site, the TCS shall be capable of planning and launching a mission within 1 hour of tasking.
ASR_Explanation: This one-hour mission planning and launch constraint drives critical architectural decisions around system performance, scalability, resource allocation, and operational orchestration.

requirement: The TCS shall be capable of operating continuously in functional Operation Mode for a minimum of 72 hours.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives the architecture’s reliability and resource-management design to ensure 72-hour continuous operation with fault tolerance and recovery.

requirement: The TCS shall have ergonomically designed operator controls and displays for the 5th percentile female to 95th percentile male operator.
ASR_Explanation: This ergonomic UI/control sizing requirement guides detailed design and hardware layout but does not drive high-level architectural decisions.

requirement: The controls shall allow the air vehicle and payload operators to perform mission control, mission monitoring, and mission updates and modifications while wearing cold weather clothing and in a Mission Oriented Protective Posture.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives hardware and UI architecture changes to ensure controls and displays remain operable with cold-weather gear and in protective posture.

requirement: The TCS shall provide the operator a caution and warning diagnostic when the TCS system has identified a malfunction.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the design of diagnostic/alerting modules, real-time event handling, and UI integration, thus affecting the system’s architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall have monitor(s) that allow reading of displays under direct sunlight and low light level environments.
ASR_Explanation: This display requirement drives the selection and integration of specialized high-brightness/low-light monitors and their supporting hardware/software subsystems.

requirement: The TCS HCI shall be a Graphical User Interface (GUI) based on X-windows and Motif.
ASR_Explanation: The mandate to use X-windows and Motif drives critical technology stack, platform dependency, and presentation‐layer architectural decisions.

requirement: When performing a given task during mission execution, the operator shall be given appropriate warning messages from other concurrently-executing subsystem tasks.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the architecture of inter-subsystem messaging, concurrency control, and operator interface notification mechanisms.

requirement: TCS Warning messages shall be color coded and flashed based on mission criticality. The color codes and flash frequencies will follow MIL-STD 1472 guidelines.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR enforces MIL-STD 1472 compliance for UI color coding and flash behavior, driving selection or design of the display framework and real-time rendering components.

requirement: The TCS operator shall be required to enter an acknowledgment prior to disabling the display of critical warning flags for any AV, Payload, ADT, GDT, and TCS faults.
ASR_Explanation: This UI-level acknowledgment requirement affects operator interface workflow without driving changes to overall system structure or architectural patterns.

requirement: Visual alerts to the TCS operator shall be in the form of a displayed message box that has a display priority greater than other existing windows to ensure that it is viewable immediately by the operator.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement dictates the UI/window-management architecture to ensure alert dialogs always render above other windows.

requirement: The position of the displayed message window shall be easily adjustable by the operator to ensure that important mission data is not obscured.
ASR_Explanation: This requires a flexible, dynamic UI layout framework in the architecture to support operator-driven repositioning of overlay windows without impacting core functionality.

requirement: In addition to displayed alert messages to the TCS operator, auditory alerts to include tones shall also be provided. The volume of these auditory tones shall be adjustable by the operator via keyboard and trackball input to at least 20dB above the speech interference level at the operator’s ear.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the audio alert subsystem design by requiring real-time volume control integration with operator input and hardware calibration.

requirement: All TCS warning messages and HCI actions shall be archived for later review.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives the design of persistent storage, logging, and audit‐trail services to support archival, indexing, and retrieval of warnings and user actions.

requirement: All TCS operator inputs shall be error checked such that any erroneous operator entry will not cause current processing to terminate. The HCI shall prompt the operator for a valid input.
ASR_Explanation: This is a UI-level input validation requirement and does not drive system-wide architectural structure or patterns.

requirement: Display jitter and flicker shall not be perceptible by the operator.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement impacts the real-time rendering pipeline and hardware/display selection to ensure sufficient refresh rates and timing control.

requirement: The TCS shall facilitate Human-Computer Interfaces (HCIs) that support operation of all system modes, functions, and capabilities.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the design and selection of the UI architecture and integration layers to ensure all system modes, functions, and capabilities are uniformly supported.

requirement: The Human Computer Interface (HCI) shall be designed and implemented in accordance with the TCS HCI Specification, TCS 108.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR constrains the UI/presentation architecture by mandating adherence to the TCS HCI Spec 108, impacting component structure and framework choices.

requirement: The HCI shall provide redundancy in all operations, so that the loss of any one HCI input device does not prohibit operation of any TCS function.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives the architecture to include redundant HCI input channels and failover mechanisms, impacting hardware/software partitioning and interface design.

requirement: The TCS shall provide the functionality to display all HCI elements on any available monitor on the TCS workstation.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the UI architecture by requiring multi-monitor support and window management across displays.

requirement: The TCS shall be capable of displaying a window within a window format to include, as a minimum, displaying a video window overlaid on a map screen as well as a map screen overlaid on a video screen.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement impacts the system’s UI/graphics architecture by imposing compositing, layering, and performance constraints on the rendering pipeline.

requirement: The TCS shall provide full complementary control operations from the keyset as well as the X/Y control device (e.g., trackball, mouse, joystick).
ASR_Explanation: Supports requirement for an input abstraction layer and event‐handling architecture to integrate both keyset and X/Y control devices.

requirement: The TCS shall provide access to the DII Style Manager so that pointing device characteristics can be modified.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement impacts the UI layer architecture by mandating integration points with the external DII Style Manager for device customization.

requirement: The shall provide the functionality to have a maximum delay time of 1 second from operator command to system acknowledgement and response.
ASR_Explanation: This sub-1s response time mandate drives performance and real-time architectural decisions around hardware sizing, concurrency, networking, and scheduling.

requirement: The TCS shall provide a capability for porting an off-the-shelf, complex control joystick with multiple toggle and multi-position switches as part of the TCS hardware suite.
ASR_Explanation: This impacts the system’s hardware abstraction and driver integration layers, requiring architectural provisions for interfacing with off-the-shelf joystick hardware.

requirement: The TCS shall use graphical representations to convey information, such as system status, C4I links, and AV-GDT links.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the selection and design of the graphical rendering framework and UI architecture to support real-time visualization and integration of system, C4I, and AV-GDT link data.

requirement: The TCS shall provide for multi-level information display tailoring by the operator.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the need for a flexible, extensible UI architecture supporting dynamic, multi‐level presentation and operator‐specific configuration.

requirement: The TCS shall provide automated TCS system information, control options, and logical & simple operator guidance and support for immediate and adaptive responding to crisis situations.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives the need for a real-time, adaptive control and guidance architecture with event-driven processing and dynamic UI/decision-support modules.

requirement: The TCS shall provide maximum automated system software support to system status monitoring and alerting of the TCS operator when a preset system parameter goes under as well as over a set threshold.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR impacts the system’s monitoring and alerting architecture by requiring support for threshold-based event detection, notification workflows, and automated status tracking.

requirement: The TCS shall provide the necessary processing, display, and control capabilities to ensure dynamic situational awareness input to the TCS operator.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the high-level design of real-time processing pipelines, display subsystems, and control integration for operator situational awareness.

requirement: The TCS shall minimize alphanumeric data display in favor of graphic, pictorial information display.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives the choice of UI/graphics framework and rendering architecture, impacting technology stack and performance requirements.

requirement: The TCS HCI shall provide unambiguous AV and payload control and status feedback indicators to ensure safe, efficient operations of two AVs and their payloads by a single TCS station.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the high-level design of real-time, concurrent control and status-feedback modules to ensure safe, efficient two-AV operations.

requirement: The TCS shall provide for a specific icon shape on a constant contrast background, as well as other visual information coding mechanisms, to cue the TCS operator regarding which UAVs are under his or her primary control.
ASR_Explanation: This is a detailed UI/UX styling requirement (icon shapes and contrast) that does not drive high-level architectural structure or technology decisions.

requirement: The TCS HCI shall provide the capability to select and amplify an object and point on a map as well as payload screen.
ASR_Explanation: This is a UI interaction detail that does not impose significant constraints on the overall system architecture.

requirement: The TCS HCI shall provide coarse and fine payload control capabilities directly on the payload screen.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the system’s UI-to-control integration by requiring new real-time state management and componentization for coarse and fine payload controls on the payload screen.

requirement: The TCS HCI shall display the SAR imaging swath on the map display.
ASR_Explanation: This is a UI‐level display requirement with no major impact on core system architecture.

requirement: The TCS HCI shall provide the on-screen capability to select and efficiently move as well as reorient a previously defined SAR imaging swath.
ASR_Explanation: It imposes architectural constraints on the UI/graphics subsystem and real-time performance requirements for interactive SAR swath manipulation.

requirement: The TCS HCI shall provide the capability to lock onto and hold a coordinate point on the payload imagery window.
ASR_Explanation: This UI interaction requirement is a localized feature that doesn’t drive major structural or cross‐cutting architectural decisions.

requirement: The TCS HCI shall provide the capability to display operator definable “Lock Out” zones around waypoints, Launch and Recovery Point (LRP), as well as any selected point on the AV flight path.
ASR_Explanation: It requires integration of geospatial mapping services and dynamic overlay rendering, impacting both UI framework and data layer architecture.

requirement: The TCS HCI shall provide for a rapid means to cancel aural warnings.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR impacts the audio/event-handling architecture by requiring a low-latency interrupt mechanism for cancelling warnings.

requirement: The TCS HCI shall provide for separation, grouping, and visual coding of multiple categories of alerts, to include Warnings, Cautions, and Advisories.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the UI architecture by mandating modular alert classification, grouping, and rendering components for visual coding.

requirement: The TCS HCI shall provide for visual Warnings, Cautions, and Advisories to be displayed at or near the center of the field of view, i.e., within a 30 degree cone, of all monitors in a TCS system.
ASR_Explanation: This is a UI placement/usability constraint for the HCI layer and does not drive fundamental architecture decisions.

requirement: For AV safety as well as mission-critical Warnings, the TCS HCI shall provide a default selection as well as an override option, along with a selection of adaptive responses, and the minimum information necessary to assist the operator in responding quickly and adaptively to the emergency.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives the HCI subsystem architecture by imposing configurable default/override flows and adaptive response logic with minimal real-time data presentation for emergency handling.

requirement: The TCS HCI shall provide for on-screen information to include, as a minimum, overlays, headers, cursors, alphanumeric annotation, waypoints, crosshairs, designed to be visible against the complete spectrum of map and payload video backgrounds.
ASR_Explanation: This impacts the UI/graphics architecture by requiring layered rendering, adaptive contrast management, and real-time performance across map and video backgrounds.

requirement: The TCS HCI shall provide continuously-available, on-screen control functions for time and mission-critical operations, to include as a minimum print, freeze, declassification, mark VCR, declutter, cease RF transmission.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives the need for a highly available, real-time UI/control architecture with redundancy and low-latency mechanisms.

requirement: The TCS HCI shall provide for the capability to automatically designate target locations from the payload screen onto the map screen.
ASR_Explanation: This is a specific UI feature requirement and does not impose major structural or quality‐attribute constraints on the system architecture.

requirement: TCS training and training support shall include the processes, procedures, techniques, training devices and equipment to train civilian, active duty and reserve military personnel to operate and support the TCS system. This will include: individual and crew training; new equipment training; initial, formal, and on-the-job training. TCS training will strike a balance between institutional, new equipment and unit training.
ASR_Explanation: This is an organizational/training process requirement and does not impose constraints on the system’s software or hardware architecture.

requirement: The TCS system shall provide, for the operator and maintainer, an embedded or add-on interactive training courseware with self-paced instruction, duplicating UAV flight performance characteristics, capabilities, and limitations.
ASR_Explanation: It requires a modular/plugin‐based architecture to host or embed a real‐time simulation engine and interactive UI that mirrors UAV flight characteristics.

requirement: The interactive courseware training capability for TCS shall be developed during Phase I and introduced to the user during scheduled demonstrations and tests.
ASR_Explanation: This is a project scheduling and delivery requirement without direct impact on the system’s architectural design.

requirement: The training capability for performance of TCS functions shall include primary mission (flight route/payload) planning, mission control and monitoring, imagery processing, tactical communications, AV control communications and TCS system on line diagnostics.
ASR_Explanation: It mandates inclusion and integration of multiple high-level modules (planning, control, processing, comms, diagnostics) impacting system decomposition and architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall provide the functional capability to train in the operation of the TCS system, performance of TCS UAV functions, and on line system troubleshooting.
ASR_Explanation: This training capability does not constrain the core system architecture and can be implemented via separate training modules or external tools.

requirement: TCS system training shall include system architecture, component familiarization, and system startup, initialization, system recovery and shutdown.
ASR_Explanation: This is a training/documentation requirement and does not impose any structural or quality-attribute constraints on the system architecture.

requirement: The TCS system shall not support formal Training operations concurrent with the execution of an actual mission. The capability for the conduct of actual communications processing concurrently with Training operations shall be provided if and only if messages are identified as training messages.
ASR_Explanation: It dictates architecture-level partitioning and concurrency control with message tagging to separate training and mission processing.

requirement: Training shall be adequate to maintain operator and maintainer skills and proficiencies.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement addresses organizational training processes and does not impose constraints on the system’s architecture.

requirement: TCS shall record operator and maintainer actions for self assessment and performance enhancement.
ASR_Explanation: It requires a cross-cutting audit/logging subsystem, impacting data storage, performance, and security design.

requirement: Operator and maintainer performance shall be measurable using parameters retrievable from the TCS to determine proficiency levels.
ASR_Explanation: It drives architectural decisions for metrics collection, storage, and analytics integration to capture and process TCS‐retrievable performance data.

requirement: Support for the TCS shall be in accordance with the Integrated Logistical Support Plan (ILSP) and the maintenance concepts and policies of the individual Services.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives the supportability and maintainability architecture—shaping diagnostic interfaces, logging frameworks, and integration with the ILSP infrastructure.

requirement: All TCS Operator Manuals and Technical Manuals shall be verified and validated prior to initial operational test.
ASR_Explanation: This is a documentation quality assurance requirement and does not drive system architecture or design decisions.

requirement: TCS transport and storage containers shall be reusable and enable the operators to set-up equipment within the established timelines in their ORDs.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement concerns physical container design and operational logistics, not the software system’s architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall adhere to DoD regulations and policy governing military standards for logistics, Petroleum, Oil and Lubricants (POL), Test, Measurement, and Diagnostic Equipment (TMDE), tools, and other support items.
ASR_Explanation: This compliance requirement imposes design constraints on data models, interoperability layers, and audit/business rules to meet DoD standards.

requirement: Standard tools, TMDE, repair parts, and lubricants shall be used to maintain the TCS. Exceptions shall be considered on a case by case basis.
ASR_Explanation: This is a maintenance process requirement concerning tooling and procedures, not one that drives system structure or major quality‐attribute trade-offs.

requirement: Each Service shall support the TCS as part of the UAV system which is organic to them.
ASR_Explanation: It mandates integration of a common telemetry/control subsystem (TCS) across all services, shaping service interfaces and deployment architecture.

requirement: The TCS shall be maintained in accordance with the UAV ORD for that Service and the level of repair analysis for the hardware chosen.
ASR_Explanation: Line 2: It drives the maintainability architecture—modular decomposition, service interfaces, and repair support—to meet UAV ORD and level-of-repair constraints.

requirement: A TCS support and fielding package shall be developed and available for operational testing.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement addresses delivery and testing support rather than imposing constraints on the system’s structure or core design decisions.

requirement: The TCS shall be maintained in accordance with Services’ approved UAV maintenance concepts and procedures.
ASR_Explanation: This is a process‐level maintenance compliance requirement and does not impose any structural or design constraints on the system architecture.

requirement: To the maximum extent possible, general purpose test equipment (GPTE) and common tools resident in each service shall be used to perform all corrective and preventative maintenance at all authorized levels of maintenance.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the design of system test interfaces and diagnostics to ensure compatibility with general purpose test equipment, impacting the testability architecture.

requirement: Tools and test equipment required to maintain the TCS but not resident in each service inventory shall be identified as special tools and special purpose test equipment (SPTE), respectively, and kept to a minimum.
