requirement: The System shall provide the capability to send an electronic notification to authorized users when they receive restricted access data.
ASR_Explanation: It requires an event-driven notification subsystem and integration with secure messaging/email services, impacting communication and security architecture.

requirement: The System shall be capable of defining Data Products for Ingest.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design of the data ingestion pipeline and metadata management services, affecting the system’s modularity and extensibility.

requirement: The System shall be capable of integrating System Elements including Scientific Algorithms, Product Tailoring Tools, Data Format Translation Tools, and other pre-defined transformation utilities.
ASR_Explanation: This drives the need for a modular, pluggable architecture with defined integration interfaces and adapter layers for diverse algorithm and tool components.

requirement: The System shall provide the capability to support functional and performance tests of system elements without impacting operational product generation.
ASR_Explanation: This requires architectural provisions for isolating test environments and integrating test harnesses to ensure tests don’t interfere with production workflows.

requirement: The System shall be capable of maintaining records of at least 30 days (configurable) worth of Ingest history.
ASR_Explanation: This retention requirement drives data storage architecture, capacity planning, and lifecycle management for ingest history.

requirement: The System shall provide the capability to control the execution of Scientific Algorithms.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the design of the core execution engine and resource management components for scheduling and integrating scientific algorithms.

requirement: The System shall be capable of generating Data Products.
ASR_Explanation: This drives the design of data ingestion, transformation, storage, and delivery layers—impacting pipeline architecture, infrastructure choices, and scalability strategies.

requirement: The System shall provide the capability for an authorized user to adjust Product Generation priorities.
ASR_Explanation: This is a routine user-level feature with minimal impact on the overall system architecture beyond standard permission checks and UI controls.

requirement: The System shall be capable of rendering Platte Carre projections.
ASR_Explanation: It requires integration of specialized cartographic projection algorithms, impacting the design of the rendering engine and graphics pipeline.

requirement: The System shall be capable of rendering Mercator projections.
ASR_Explanation: It dictates the choice of rendering engine and GIS libraries, impacting core mapping architecture and performance.

requirement: The System shall be capable of rendering Polar Stereographic projections.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement impacts the core mapping engine design by mandating integration of a Polar Stereographic projection library and associated data transformation pipeline.

requirement: The System shall be capable of aggregating Data Products up to and including one orbit.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives key architectural decisions around data ingestion throughput, storage capacity, and batch aggregation pipeline design to handle orbit-scale data volumes.

requirement: The System shall be capable of producing time-averaged Data Products.
ASR_Explanation: This is a specific data‐processing feature with minimal impact on high‐level architectural structure.

requirement: The System shall be capable of generating NOAA-Unique and Tailored Data Products with Government-specified grid spacing.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design of specialized spatial data processing and grid-generation components, impacting the system’s data modeling and integration architecture.

requirement: The System shall be capable of reformatting Data Products into BUFR.
ASR_Explanation: This is a straightforward data format conversion that can be implemented within existing processing modules without altering the overall architecture.

requirement: The System shall be capable of reformatting Data Products into GRIB2.
ASR_Explanation: This requires integrating a GRIB2 encoding layer and will impact the data processing pipeline, component interfaces, and library dependencies.

requirement: The System shall be capable of reformatting Data Products into GeoTIFF.
ASR_Explanation: This is a straightforward data-format conversion feature that can be implemented within the existing processing pipeline without impacting overall system structure.

requirement: The System shall be capable of reformatting Data Products into HDF5.
ASR_Explanation: Integrating HDF5 conversion drives choice of data‐processing modules and external libraries, impacting module boundaries and dependency management.

requirement: The System shall be capable of reformatting Data Products into netCDF.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the integration of netCDF I/O libraries and shapes the data‐transformation pipeline and module interfaces.

requirement: The System shall be capable of compressing Data Products with the GZIP algorithm.
ASR_Explanation: Specifying GZIP compression drives the inclusion of a compression module and impacts data storage, processing performance, and network throughput.

requirement: The System shall be capable of compressing Data Products with the ZIP algorithm.
ASR_Explanation: Specifying ZIP compression is a low-level implementation detail with minimal impact on the overall system architecture.

requirement: The System shall be capable of compressing Data Products with the RICE algorithm.
ASR_Explanation: Specifying the RICE algorithm for data compression dictates component design, performance tuning, and integration of specific libraries.

requirement: The System shall be capable of compressing images of Data Products with the JPEG algorithm.
ASR_Explanation: Specifying JPEG compression constrains the choice of image-processing components and impacts pipeline design and performance optimization.

requirement: The System shall be capable of compressing animated images of Data Products with the MPEG algorithm.
ASR_Explanation: This is a low‐level implementation detail (choice of compression algorithm) that can be handled within a module without altering the overall system architecture.

requirement: The System shall be capable of generating Data Products within a time frame not to exceed the maximum user-specified shelf-life of the Data Product.
ASR_Explanation: It imposes strict end-to-end latency bounds on data product generation, driving architectural decisions around performance optimization, scheduling, and scalable resource allocation.

requirement: The System shall be capable of making Data Products available to registered users.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard functional requirement for user access and data delivery without major architectural impact.

requirement: The System shall provide an online capability for registered users to request subscriptions to xDRs, Ancillary, NOAA-Unique, and Tailored Data Products.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard subscription-management feature with routine user authentication and request handling, not driving major architectural changes.

requirement: The System shall provide an online capability for the Government to approve subscription requests for xDRs, Ancillary, NOAA-Unique, and Tailored Data Products.
ASR_Explanation: Line 2: It defines a standard approval workflow and role-based access but doesn’t impose fundamental architectural constraints on structure, performance, or integration.

requirement: The System shall be capable of distributing NOAA-Unique Data Products to the CLASS Long-Term Archive (LTA).
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design of external integration interfaces, data transfer mechanisms, and reliability components for archiving.

requirement: The System shall provide a list of files distributed to CLASS.
ASR_Explanation: This is a routine file‐listing feature with no significant impact on the overall system architecture.

requirement: The System shall provide the capability to receive an electronic delivery acknowledgement message from CLASS.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the integration architecture by defining the external messaging interface and protocol handling for CLASS acknowledgements.

requirement: The System shall be capable of delivering NDE Data Products to registered user's computer systems.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives architectural decisions for secure, scalable data delivery pipelines, network protocols, and integration with user systems.

requirement: The System shall be capable of notifying registered users of the status of their orders.
ASR_Explanation: This requires designing an event-driven notification subsystem with messaging infrastructure and integration points, impacting overall system scalability and reliability.

requirement: The System shall be capable of notifying registered users of the status of the System.
ASR_Explanation: It introduces the need for a notification service or messaging infrastructure to deliver status updates, impacting system integration and scalability.

requirement: The System shall ensure that NDE information is accessible only to those authorized to have access.
ASR_Explanation: It mandates integration of authentication/authorization mechanisms and data protection services, impacting security architecture and access control design.

requirement: The System shall be capable of registering Users for the purpose of controlling their access to System Elements and Data Products.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the design of the identity management and access control architecture, impacting security modules, data stores, and integration points.

requirement: The System shall provide a mechanism for reporting performance related to resource utilization, communications, product latencies, and hardware.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the need for a monitoring/telemetry infrastructure and instrumentation across components to collect, store, and report performance metrics.

requirement: The System shall provide an automatic failover capability that will re-create a fully functioning configuration from a failed configuration.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the system’s high-availability architecture by mandating redundancy, failover mechanisms, and state replication strategies.

requirement: In the event of a failure of the Operational Environment during the NPOESS missions, the System Test Environment shall be capable of delivering products to the operational community within the same throughput and latency parameters as the operational system.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives a high-availability, production-equivalent failover architecture requiring matching throughput and latency, impacting infrastructure design and capacity planning.

requirement: The System shall be capable of monitoring the integrity of all data received from external sources.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the architecture of data ingestion pipelines and security/monitoring components to ensure integrity checks, logging, and alerting for all external inputs.

requirement: The System shall provide the capability for an authorized user to define data input integrity checks.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the need for a configurable, rule-based validation framework and secure administration interface, impacting system modularity and extensibility.

requirement: The System shall be capable of executing and recording the results of data input integrity checks, and initiate actions based on those results.
ASR_Explanation: It introduces cross-cutting validation, audit logging, and event-driven triggers that impact component layering and module boundaries.

requirement: The System shall be capable of making products available for distribution according to customer-specified latencies.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement imposes per-customer latency constraints that will drive architectural decisions around performance, scheduling, and scalability.

requirement: The System shall have the capability to monitor, record statistics, and deliver reports on its performance.
ASR_Explanation: It introduces cross-cutting monitoring and reporting concerns that impact decisions on instrumentation, metrics storage, data pipelines, and service integration.

requirement: The System shall be capable of retaining logs of all performance data for no less than 90 consecutive calendar days.
ASR_Explanation: Impacts the storage and log management architecture by requiring sizing, retention policies, and archival mechanisms for 90-day data storage.

requirement: The System shall provide the capability to generate custom reports from data collected during data receipt, processing, and distribution.
ASR_Explanation: Providing custom reporting impacts the data storage schema, reporting engine integration, and performance/scalability architecture.

requirement: The System shall be capable of receiving data and products from IDPS.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the system’s integration architecture by defining external interfaces, data ingestion pipelines, and protocol/security considerations for connecting to the IDPS.

requirement: The System shall be capable of requesting and accepting all xDRs generated by the IDPS.
ASR_Explanation: It mandates a high-throughput, secure data ingestion interface and scalable storage architecture for xDRs.

requirement: The System shall be capable of receiving SARSAT Telemetry from the IDPS.
ASR_Explanation: Integration of SARSAT telemetry dictates design of communication interfaces, data ingestion pipelines, and real-time processing infrastructure.

requirement: The System shall be capable of receiving A-DCS data and telemetry from the IDPS.
ASR_Explanation: It dictates integration and data ingestion architecture, requiring support for specific telemetry protocols, interfaces, and buffering components.

requirement: The System shall provide an operator console capable of utilizing IDPS client software for submitting subscriptions for Data Products to the IDPS.
ASR_Explanation: Mandates integration with an external IDPS client affecting system interfaces, component boundaries, and deployment architecture.

requirement: The System shall be capable of automatically submitting Ad Hoc Requests to the IDPS for retransmission of Data Products.
ASR_Explanation: Requires integration with the IDPS via new asynchronous messaging APIs, impacting the data pipeline, error handling, and scheduling components.

requirement: The System shall be capable of receiving Data Delivery Reports (DDRs) from the IDPS.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement impacts the integration architecture by defining external interface protocols, message ingestion pipelines, and reliability/performance considerations for DDR handling.

requirement: The System shall be capable of receiving Ancillary Data Products from the Environmental Satellite Processing Center (ESPC).
ASR_Explanation: This integration requirement drives the system’s external interface and data ingestion architecture, including communication protocols and reliability considerations.

requirement: The System shall be capable of ingesting and processing archived data.
ASR_Explanation: This impacts the overall data pipeline and storage architecture by driving decisions around ingestion frameworks, batch/stream processing, and scalability.

requirement: The System shall provide an operator console capable of requesting Data Products from the Comprehensive Large Array-data Stewardship System (CLASS).
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard functional requirement for a user interface action with minimal impact on the overall system architecture.

requirement: The System shall be capable of receiving Data Products delivered from CLASS.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement impacts the system’s integration architecture by defining external interface, data ingestion pipeline, and associated protocols.

requirement: The System shall be capable of sending a Data Receipt to CLASS for each Data Product received from CLASS.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard acknowledgment requirement that can be fulfilled within the existing messaging/integration framework without driving major architectural changes.

requirement: ESPC Operations shall provide an interface between NDE and the NPOESS Mission Management Center (MMC) such that 100% of the NDE inquiries to the MMC and NDE replies to MMC requests are received by the MMC in a time not to exceed that specified in the ICD, and that 100% of the notifications and inquiries from the MMC to NDE are received by NDE in a time not to exceed that specified by the ICD.
ASR_Explanation: This imposes strict end-to-end latency and reliability constraints on the NDE–MMC interface, driving architecture choices in messaging middleware, protocols, and network QoS.

requirement: The System shall provide an operator console capable of interfacing with the NPOESS trouble ticketing system.
ASR_Explanation: It necessitates defining integration components and communication protocols for interfacing with the external NPOESS ticketing system.

requirement: The System shall provide an operator console capable of interfacing with the ESPC trouble ticketing system.
ASR_Explanation: It requires defining an integration layer and adapter patterns for interfacing the operator console with the external ESPC trouble ticketing system.

requirement: The System shall be capable of executing tasks according to a schedule.
ASR_Explanation: It mandates a scheduling subsystem for timed task execution, impacting component structure and infrastructure decisions.

requirement: The System shall be capable of executing tasks initiated by events.
ASR_Explanation: This drives the need for an event‐driven architecture and asynchronous messaging infrastructure.

requirement: The System shall be capable of executing 99% of its scheduled tasks in any consecutive 30 day period.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the architecture of the scheduling subsystem by requiring high reliability, fault tolerance, retry mechanisms, and monitoring for 99% task completion.

requirement: The System shall be capable of executing 99% of its event driven tasks in any consecutive 30 day period.
ASR_Explanation: This reliability target drives architectural decisions around fault tolerance, redundancy, monitoring, and event-driven processing scalability.

requirement: The System shall be capable of adjusting the priorities of scheduled and event-driven tasks.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the design of the task scheduling and concurrency mechanisms by requiring flexible priority management in the architecture.

requirement: The System shall have the capacity to store 4 Terabytes (TBs) of data for testing purposes.
ASR_Explanation: This 4 TB storage requirement drives the design and sizing of the storage subsystem and underlying data infrastructure.

requirement: The System shall be capable of reporting on all ingested, generated, and distributed data according to the (Federal Geographic Data Committee (FGDC) Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata (CSDGM), Vers. 2 (FGDC-STD-001-1998) and the CSDGM Remote Sensing Extensions (FGDC-STD-012-2002).
ASR_Explanation: This NFR imposes strict compliance with FGDC metadata standards, impacting the data model, reporting components, and integration architecture.

requirement: The System shall be capable of storing product quality information in the metadata of products created.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement impacts the data model and metadata storage architecture by requiring schema extensions and retrieval mechanisms for product quality information.

requirement: The System shall be capable of retaining Data Products for a configurable period of time not to exceed 96 hours.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the design of data storage and lifecycle management components, enforcing configurable retention and automated cleanup mechanisms.

requirement: The System shall be capable of accepting technology updates without System downtime.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the architecture toward zero-downtime deployment patterns (e.g., blue-green/rolling upgrades, service decoupling, load-balanced clusters).

requirement: The System shall be capable of adding additional capacity without redesign of its infrastructure.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives the need for a scalable, modular architecture (e.g., horizontal scaling or elastic resource management) to add capacity without redesign.

requirement: The System shall comply with ESPC (DoC/NOAA/NESDIS) procedures and policies as stated in the NOAA IT Security Manual 212-1300, Version 3.3, dated May 15, 2007.
ASR_Explanation: It imposes security and compliance constraints on the system architecture, requiring specific controls, policy enforcement, and audit mechanisms.

requirement: The System shall be capable of generating backups for all NDE data, procedures, and software.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design of the system’s backup and recovery architecture, storage infrastructure, and data consistency mechanisms.

requirement: The System shall be capable of implementing automated backup procedures.
ASR_Explanation: It drives data storage and replication design, scheduling mechanisms, and disaster recovery infrastructure.

requirement: The System shall be capable of returning to nominal operations following a restoration from system backups.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives the design of backup/restore workflows, state consistency mechanisms, and availability/recovery strategies.

requirement: The System shall be capable of protecting the integrity of NDE data in the event of human errors.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives the design of data integrity and recovery mechanisms (validation, transaction management, audit) across the system architecture.

requirement: The System shall be capable of protecting the integrity of NDE data in the event of computer to computer transmission errors.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the design of communication protocols and error‐detection/correction mechanisms, impacting the system’s network and data‐integrity architecture.

requirement: The System shall be capable of protecting the integrity of NDE data from software bugs.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the data and error-handling architecture by mandating validation, transactional controls, and recovery mechanisms to protect NDE data integrity.

requirement: The System shall be capable of protecting the integrity of NDE data in the event of a hardware malfunction such as a disk crash.
ASR_Explanation: Line 2: Ensuring data integrity on hardware failure drives the inclusion of redundant storage, backups, and fault-tolerant mechanisms in the system architecture.

requirement: The System shall be capable of protecting the integrity of NDE data in the event of a natural disaster.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR influences the overall system architecture by requiring disaster recovery, geo-redundant data replication, and backup strategies to ensure data integrity.

requirement: The NDE System can be accessed only with the authorization of the Government.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the security architecture by requiring integration with government authorization mechanisms for access control.

requirement: The System shall be constructed using existing hardware where it is possible, practical, and approved by the Government. Inventory will be provided to the Contractor by OSDPD IT Lead.
ASR_Explanation: This hardware reuse constraint directly impacts platform selection, performance, compatibility, and overall system architecture decisions.

requirement: The System shall be constructed using existing software where it is possible, practical, and approved by the Government. Inventory will be provided to the Contractor by OSDPD IT Lead.
ASR_Explanation: Constrains technology selection and integration architecture by mandating reuse of existing, approved software components.

requirement: The System shall be constructed using COTS and Open Source software where it is possible, practical, and approved by the Government.
ASR_Explanation: This constraint on using COTS and OSS directly impacts technology selection, licensing, and integration strategies in the system architecture.

requirement: The NDE System Elements shall be designed to be reused in other Satellite Data Processing applications.
ASR_Explanation: This NFR drives modularity and componentization decisions to ensure interfaces and design patterns support reuse across Satellite Data Processing applications.

requirement: The NDE System shall be designed so that Scientific Algorithms are invoked as objects.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts core system architecture by enforcing an object-oriented plugin model for algorithm integration.

requirement: The System shall provide the capability for operators to log and track events (i.e., MMC Service Requests, MMC Service Request Responses).
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard functional logging/tracking feature with minimal impact on overall system architecture.

requirement: The System shall provide the capability for operators to update a summary log for the purposes of tracking the occurrence of significant events or activities.
ASR_Explanation: This is a routine logging feature with minimal impact on overall system architecture.

requirement: The System shall provide the capability for operators to sort and search on summary log events for the purposes of identifying, troubleshooting, and reporting on significant activities.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the design of logging storage, indexing, and search subsystems impacting data architecture and performance.

requirement: The System shall be capable of reporting on summary log events.
ASR_Explanation: This is a detailed logging/reporting feature that doesn’t drive high-level architectural structure.

requirement: The System shall provide the capability for operators to quiesce and restart the System. This capability should be designed to minimize interruptions of service.
ASR_Explanation: This impacts availability and operational architecture by requiring design for graceful shutdown, state persistence, and restart orchestration to minimize downtime.

requirement: The System shall implement operational quality monitoring procedures that will detect and report to operators and management the acceptability of xDR metadata according to configurable thresholds.
ASR_Explanation: It requires integrating a monitoring and alerting subsystem with configurable thresholds, impacting component interfaces, data flows, and infrastructure choices.

requirement: The System shall implement operational quality monitoring procedures that will detect and report to operators and management the acceptability of output product metadata according to configurable thresholds.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the addition of a monitoring and reporting subsystem with configurable thresholds, impacting the system’s modules, data flow, and operator interfaces.

requirement: The System shall be capable of changing system status to reflect the condition of NPOESS (i.e., Degraded Operations, Restricted Access).
ASR_Explanation: Requires a core state machine to handle operational modes and propagate status changes across components.

requirement: The System shall provide the capability for operators to forward NDE System Elements to CLASS for permanent archive.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the need for an external archive interface, impacting data flow, integration patterns, and storage architecture.

requirement: The NDE Ingest capability shall not be interrupted for more than 2 hours in any consecutive 24 hour period and no more than 4 hours in any consecutive 30 day period.
ASR_Explanation: This uptime requirement drives the need for high-availability architecture with redundancy, failover, and monitoring to meet strict availability SLAs.

requirement: The NDE Product Generation capability shall not be interrupted for more than 2 hours in any consecutive 24 hour period and no more than 4 hours in any consecutive 30 day period.
ASR_Explanation: This high-availability requirement drives architectural decisions around redundancy, failover, and monitoring to meet strict uptime targets.

requirement: The NDE Distribution capability shall not be interrupted for more than 2 hours in any consecutive 24 hour period and no more than 4 hours in any consecutive 30 day period.
ASR_Explanation: This availability requirement drives architectural decisions around redundancy, failover, monitoring, and resilience.

requirement: The NDE Operations Monitoring capability shall not be interrupted for more than 2 hours in any consecutive 24 hour period and no more than 4 hours in any consecutive 30 day period.
ASR_Explanation: This availability constraint drives architecture decisions around high availability, redundancy, failover, and disaster recovery strategies.

requirement: The Development and Maintenance Environment shall be available to at least 25 developers 85% of the time during normal business hours over any consecutive twelve month period. Normal business hours are 8am to 4pm Eastern Time, Monday through Friday.
ASR_Explanation: This uptime requirement drives architectural decisions around high availability, redundancy, capacity planning, and monitoring for the development environment.

requirement: The System shall be capable of accessing data delivered by IDPS within no more than ten seconds of notification from IDPS.
ASR_Explanation: This ten-second latency requirement drives architecture choices for real-time data integration, processing pipelines, and performance optimization.

requirement: The System shall deliver SARSAT Telemetry data from IDPS to US Mission Control Center (USMCC) within 1 minute of their receipt.
ASR_Explanation: This latency-bound delivery requirement drives architectural decisions around real-time data pipelines, network QoS, and performance scalability.

requirement: The System shall deliver A-DCS Telemetry data from IDPS to the US Global Processing Center within 1 minute of their receipt.
ASR_Explanation: This strict end-to-end latency requirement will drive choices in data transport, network topology, queuing systems, and resource provisioning.

requirement: The System shall deliver A-DCS Data Products from IDPS to the US Global Processing Center within 1 minute of their receipt.
ASR_Explanation: This latency requirement drives architectural decisions around real-time data pipelines, network infrastructure, and processing performance.

requirement: During the NPP mission, the System shall be capable of supporting product volumes of 4 Terabytes per day of input and 8 Terabytes per day of output.
ASR_Explanation: This throughput requirement drives the design of storage, processing, and network scalability to handle high daily data volumes.

requirement: During the NPOESS-C1 mission, the System shall be capable of supporting product volumes of 8 Terabytes per day of input and 16 Terabytes per day of output.
ASR_Explanation: This data volume requirement drives critical decisions on storage architecture, network throughput, and processing scalability.

requirement: During the NPOESS-C2 mission, the System shall be capable of supporting product volumes of 8 Terabytes per day of input and 16 Terabytes per day of output.
ASR_Explanation: This high data volume requirement drives key architectural decisions around storage capacity, throughput, network bandwidth, and scalable processing infrastructure.

requirement: The System shall be compliant with the OMB Federal Enterprise Architecture (FEA) according to the documents dated December 2006, and available from http://www.whitehouse.gov/omb/egov/a-2-EAModelsNEW2.html.
ASR_Explanation: This compliance requirement will drive the overall enterprise architecture design, enforcing adherence to FEA reference models, standards, and governance.

requirement: The user shall be able to view the data stored in the Physical Inventory tables through the Pontis graphical user interface (GUI).
ASR_Explanation: This is a basic UI data‐display requirement with no significant impact on core architectural patterns or system structure.

requirement: The user shall be able to switch between predefined structure lists.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard UI navigation feature that does not drive changes in the overall system architecture.

requirement: The user shall be able to select structures by structure ID, district, county, administrative area (geographic group), ownership, custodianship, functional class, NHS status, on/off system, inspector, bridge group, inspection due dates, and all or some environment and elements. The query shall be restricted depending on the user privilege.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard multi-criteria filtering and authorization requirement with no major impact on the overall system architecture.

requirement: The user shall be able to find structures based on the structure ID, structure name, feature intersected, facility carried, route number, LRS Inventory Route, KM/mile posts, structure types, design types and materials, and by searching the notes fields. The query shall be restricted depending on the user privilege.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the design of the data access/search layer (indexing/query optimization) and enforces row-level security based on user privileges.

requirement: The user shall be able to enter a WHERE clause of the SQL query in the Find and the Filter window and run a query against the bridges in the database. The query will be verified by the application and SQL errors will be reported to the user for correction. This capability shall be an application privilege. Bridge-level security of the user shall be applied automatically.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design of the data‐access layer, requiring dynamic SQL parsing/validation, privilege management, and bridge‐level security enforcement.

requirement: The user shall be able to select a structure by directly typing in the structure ID in GUI, with the display scrolling to that structure. Bridge-level security of the user shall be applied automatically.
ASR_Explanation: This is a straightforward UI behavior with routine security enforcement and does not drive a new or altered architectural approach.

requirement: The system shall display the data in the appropriate unit of measurement as configured for the application. This will be a permanent modal setting for the application. This setting shall be configurable (as stipulated in Requirement 22.1).
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard UI configuration requirement with minimal impact on system architecture.

requirement: The user shall be able to select bridges on a supported map display and generate a bridge list for use within Pontis, for example, to perform a simulation analysis or to generate an inspection roster.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard UI feature with routine data export to Pontis and does not drive a major architectural decision.

requirement: The user shall be able to view the data stored in the Project Planning tables through the GUI.
ASR_Explanation: This is a routine functional requirement for data viewing and does not impose significant architectural constraints.

requirement: The user shall be able to switch between predefined project lists.
ASR_Explanation: This is a straightforward UI feature with no significant impact on system-level architecture or design patterns.

requirement: The user shall be able to filter and select projects by structure ID, project ID, program, action type, project status, review status, treatment, program year, and project end date.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard multi-criteria filtering requirement that can be handled via existing API query parameters and database indexing without altering the overall architecture.

requirement: The user shall be able to find projects by project ID, project name, project status, structure ID, and program.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard data retrieval feature that can be handled with routine database indexing and query mechanisms.

requirement: The user shall be able to filter and select work candidates by structure ID, project ID, program, action type, project status, review status, treatment, program year, and project end date.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the data access and storage architecture by requiring efficient multi‐attribute indexing or search infrastructure to support complex filtering.

requirement: The user shall be able to enter a WHERE clause of the SQL query in the Find and the Filter window and run a query against the projects in the database. The query will be verified by the application and SQL errors will be reported to the user for correction. This capability shall be an application privilege. Bridge-level security of the user shall be applied automatically.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement impacts the data‐access architecture by introducing dynamic SQL parsing/validation and enforcing security/privilege checks at the bridge level.

requirement: The user shall be able to select a project by directly typing in the project ID in the GUI, with the display scrolling to that structure. Bridge or project level security of the user shall be applied automatically.
ASR_Explanation: This is a UI-level interaction that can leverage the existing security framework without requiring changes to the overall system architecture.

requirement: The user shall be able to select projects (work) on a supported map display and generate a project/bridge list for use within Pontis, for example, to display results of a simulation analysis or to generate a STIP display.
ASR_Explanation: The requirement drives integration of a GIS map component with Pontis data export, impacting system modules and data exchange architecture.

requirement: The user shall be able to select and execute predefined reports to view bridge data, project data, preservation model data, program simulation results, and configuration data. The existing Pontis reports shall form the basis for the predefined reports to be provided in Pontis 5.0.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard functional reporting feature that does not drive core architectural decisions.

requirement: The user shall be able to create reports in a .NET compliant report generator other than InfoMaker and review these reports from within the Pontis GUI.
ASR_Explanation: This impacts the system’s architecture by requiring an abstraction/plugin layer for .NET report generators and integration points in the Pontis GUI.

requirement: The system shall provide the ability to integrate current selections with a .NET compliant report generator other than InfoMaker. AASHTO may provide users with a list of suggested report generators to replace PowerBuilder/InfoMaker.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement mandates designing pluggable reporting adapters and interface abstractions to support multiple .NET‐compliant generators, impacting module boundaries and integration strategy.

requirement: The system shall provide the ability to incorporate PDF files into standard Pontis reports.
ASR_Explanation: Incorporating PDFs into reports impacts the reporting subsystem architecture by requiring a PDF‐processing component and associated library integrations.

requirement: The system shall provide the ability to save reports in PDF, HTML, and XML formats.
ASR_Explanation: This is a specific report‐generation detail that can be handled within existing modules without driving high‐level architectural changes.

requirement: The system shall provide the ability to create any report that is bridge or project based by selecting the relevant “driver” records from a map display.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives integration of a GIS-based UI with dynamic report generation and data-access services, impacting core architectural modules.

requirement: The system shall provide the ability to create or refresh a map display from a set of bridge or project records selected within the Pontis desktop.
ASR_Explanation: This is a straightforward UI feature for map rendering with minimal impact on the overall system architecture.

requirement: The user shall be able to create a new structure with a user-defined bridge key.
ASR_Explanation: This is a localized functional detail that does not drive high-level architectural structure or cross-cutting concerns.

requirement: The user shall be able to create a new structure by copying all data of an existing structure.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard CRUD operation with minimal impact on overall architecture.

requirement: The user shall be able to create new structure(s) by importing from a supported file type. The supported file types are to be determined, but will include at least PDI, NBI, and XML.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design of an extensible file‐import and parsing module, affecting the system’s modularity and integration layers.

requirement: The user shall be able to edit and remove existing structures.
ASR_Explanation: This CRUD requirement is a standard functionality change that doesn’t impact the overall system architecture or design patterns.

requirement: The system shall provide the ability to mark a bridge with any of several status values (e.g. inactive/closed) and automatically filter displays to recognize the status value (e.g. to automatically suppress inactive/closed structures).
ASR_Explanation: This is a straightforward data‐model and UI filtering enhancement with minimal impact on the overall system architecture.

requirement: The system shall support storage and management of information for bridges in a design or preconstruction state.
ASR_Explanation: This core domain requirement will drive the data model, storage design, and service boundaries in the system architecture.

requirement: The user shall be able to renumber the bridge key identifier and have the change made globally. This functionality shall be restricted depending on the user privilege.
ASR_Explanation: This drives the design of global data propagation and permission enforcement mechanisms, impacting system consistency and security architecture.

requirement: The user shall be able to create a new inspection.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard CRUD operation with no notable impact on the overall system architecture.

requirement: The user shall be able to create a new inspection by copying a previous inspection.
ASR_Explanation: This is a straightforward CRUD enhancement (copying data) and does not drive major architectural decisions.

requirement: The user shall be able to identify and select any previous inspection as the source for the copy action. The selection can be made by combination of date, inspection type, inspector, or inspection control group.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard data‐filtering feature affecting the UI and query layer without imposing major architectural changes.

requirement: The system shall have a short-form interface (display only NBI and element data, similar to SI&A sheet) for inspection data entry with specifics of design and functionality to be determined as part of the technical design.
ASR_Explanation: This is a UI-level feature definition and does not impose constraints on the high-level system architecture.

requirement: The user shall have the option to copy the notes from previous inspection when creating a new inspection.
ASR_Explanation: This is a localized UI/data feature for copying notes with minimal architectural impact.

requirement: The user shall be able to create new inspection(s) by importing a PDI file.
ASR_Explanation: This is a localized file‐import feature that doesn’t impose new cross‐cutting or infrastructure‐level architectural constraints.

requirement: The user shall be able to add new elements to the structure.
ASR_Explanation: This is a basic CRUD operation with minimal architectural impact.

requirement: The user shall be able to edit all the NBI data.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard CRUD capability with minimal impact on high-level architecture.

requirement: Changes to calculated results such as Sufficiency Rating or NBI Appraisal Ratings will be prohibited except for secured, privileged users.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement imposes a need for a robust authentication/authorization layer and privilege management across application, API, and data storage.

requirement: The user shall be able to edit element inspection data.
ASR_Explanation: This is standard CRUD functionality with no significant impact on the overall system architecture.

requirement: The user shall be able to edit the bridge inventory items excluding the dependent/derived values (i.e., the appraisal ratings that are calculated automatically).
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard CRUD business rule affecting UI/form behavior and service logic without driving a new architectural pattern.

requirement: The user shall be able to edit inspection and structure notes.
ASR_Explanation: This is a basic CRUD requirement that won’t significantly affect the overall system architecture beyond standard data storage and UI handling.

requirement: The system shall be capable of storing notes in plain text and XML, and shall maximize the notes field size. The user shall be capable of setting the size of the notes field.
ASR_Explanation: This only affects data storage field sizing and format support at the database/schema level, not the overall system architecture.

requirement: The user shall be able to create and edit work recommendations. The system shall be capable of estimating quantity for bridge type work recommendations.
ASR_Explanation: It introduces a specialized bridge quantity-estimation module that affects domain modeling, component decomposition, and performance considerations.

requirement: The user shall be able to enter and update inspection planning information (i.e., frequency of regular and special inspections and estimated resource requirements).
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard CRUD operation for entering and updating data with no significant architectural impact.

requirement: The user shall be able to link and manage multimedia files.
ASR_Explanation: This FR drives the need for a multimedia management subsystem—impacting storage architecture, metadata indexing, processing pipelines, and delivery mechanisms.

requirement: The system shall provide an inspection scheduling tool.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the design of a dedicated scheduling service with calendar integration, event-driven workflows and resource management, shaping service boundaries and data models.

requirement: The system shall provide the capability to “lock down” an inspection record once signed and sealed to prevent deletion or modification.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement affects the data storage and security architecture by mandating immutable, write-once record handling and audit-trail enforcement.

requirement: Privileged users shall be able to delete an existing inspection unless it is “locked down”.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement affects the system’s security architecture and data model by imposing role-based access control and state-based deletion rules.

requirement: The user shall be able to sort the element data by unit number, environment, and quantity.
ASR_Explanation: This sorting requirement impacts UI functionality but does not drive core architectural decisions.

requirement: The user shall be able to view all data from any two inspections, for a single bridge, in a tiled presentation for easy comparison. The presentation (view/report) shall clearly highlight the difference between two inspections.
ASR_Explanation: This is a localized UI presentation requirement with minimal impact on the overall system architecture.

requirement: The system shall provide a configurable tracking mechanism to ensure that a set of screens and data fields have been reviewed and approved during the course of an inspection.
ASR_Explanation: It introduces a cross-cutting configurable audit and approval workflow requiring design of a persistent tracking and configuration subsystem.

requirement: The system shall be capable of calculating NBI condition ratings using the FHWA NBI Translator.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives integration with an external FHWA NBI Translator component, impacting the system’s interface and data processing architecture.

requirement: The system shall be capable of calculating and storing bridge Sufficiency Rating.
ASR_Explanation: Introducing Sufficiency Rating calculation and storage will affect the system’s data model, processing engine, and persistence architecture.

requirement: The system shall be capable of calculating appraisal ratings.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard business‐logic feature and does not impose high‐level architectural constraints.

requirement: The system shall be capable of calculating Structurally Deficient/Functionally Obsolete (SD/FO) status.
ASR_Explanation: This is a straightforward functional capability without significant impact on system structure or quality attributes.

requirement: The ability of the user to update the calculated ratings and status in the database shall be a controlled privilege. Agencies may optionally exclude/prohibit direct edits to the calculated inspection results such as the NBI translator output. The agency shall therefore be able to enforce strict use of calculated translator results for calculating NBI condition ratings.
ASR_Explanation: Requires implementing a configurable access-control layer to enforce strict editing privileges on calculated inspection results.

requirement: The system shall be capable of prompting users when the dependent fields for ratings are changed and offer the user option to recalculate the ratings.
ASR_Explanation: This is a localized UI interaction requirement with minimal impact on the overall system architecture.

requirement: The system administrator shall be able to configure the recalculation behavior of the derived data. The options shall include 1) do nothing, 2) alert user that recalculation is required and give option to recalculate before update/save, and 3) recalculate silently with no confirmation prompt when user saves the inspection.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the need for a configurable strategy/plug‐in in the data processing pipeline, impacting core business logic and configuration management.

requirement: The system shall eliminate any and all dependencies on workstation (user) control/parameter files for program operation. For example, parameter information required by the NBI Translator (including information currently stored in ELEMENTS.PRN and ERRORS.LST) will be stored strictly within the Pontis database rather than on individual user workstations.
ASR_Explanation: Centralizing parameter storage in the database impacts data storage and access architecture, requiring redesign of configuration management and deployment strategy.

requirement: The system shall be capable of creating cost and deterioration elicitation records for Pontis users.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard CRUD functionality for data entry with minimal impact on the overall system architecture.

requirement: The system shall be capable of updating the transition probabilities for preservation actions based on expert elicitations.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the core decision‐model design by requiring a runtime update mechanism for transition probabilities, affecting data models, service APIs, and persistence.

requirement: The system shall be capable of updating preservation action costs based on expert elicitations.
ASR_Explanation: This is a routine business function affecting domain logic and data updates without driving high-level architectural structure.

requirement: The system shall be capable of updating transition probabilities for do-nothing actions based on historical inspection data.
ASR_Explanation: It’s a detailed functional behavior that doesn’t drive high-level architectural structure or quality attribute decisions.

requirement: The user shall be able to edit the cost and deterioration models through the GUI and immediately see the impact on model recommendations.
ASR_Explanation: It requires an event-driven, real-time update architecture for on-the-fly model recalculation and UI/backend integration.

requirement: The system shall be capable of using the transition probabilities and preservation action costs to develop an optimal preservation policy as detailed in the Pontis Technical Manual.
ASR_Explanation: It dictates the inclusion and design of a decision-making engine and data model for computing optimal preservation policies, impacting system modularity and performance.

requirement: The system shall be capable of recovering the model if the elicitation process fails.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the architecture of fault-tolerance and recovery subsystems, influencing persistence, transaction/rollback, and error-handling design.

requirement: The system shall be capable of restoring the previous action costs, transition probabilities, and preservation policy.
ASR_Explanation: This impacts the persistence and state‐management architecture by requiring design of storage, serialization, and recovery mechanisms for action costs, transition data, and policies.

requirement: The system shall be capable of supporting multiple cost and deterioration models.
ASR_Explanation: It requires a modular, extensible architecture (e.g., plugin/strategy patterns) to support interchangeable cost and deterioration models.

requirement: The system shall be capable of incorporating Health Index targets in a preservation (short-term) model.
ASR_Explanation: Incorporating Health Index targets will require extending the preservation engine’s data schema, calculation modules, and configuration/integration layers.

requirement: The system shall streamline the model generation process (i.e. remove any unnecessary prompts as currently implemented in version 4.x).
ASR_Explanation: This is a localized functional improvement in the model generation workflow without requiring major structural or platform changes.

requirement: The system shall be capable of determining the long-term cost of achieving an average health index target for one or more structure(s), broken down by elements, element categories, for the entire structure.
ASR_Explanation: This is a functional calculation requirement that doesn’t impose structural, performance, or non-functional constraints driving major architectural decisions.

requirement: The system shall provide a preservation model dashboard integrating the capabilities described above. The exact functionality of the dashboard will be informed by review of the results of NCHRP Project 12-67, Multiple-Objective Optimization for Bridge Management Systems.
ASR_Explanation: This drives architectural decisions around building a dynamic dashboard UI, integrating optimization results data flows, and service‐layer design.

requirement: The user shall be able to specify the characteristics of the simulation, including the simulation timeframe, various thresholds for the simulation, the project types included in the simulation, the needs addressed, and the annual budget for the simulation.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard UI-driven configuration feature with minimal impact on overall system architecture.

requirement: The user shall be able to update the unit costs and the policy standards governing the improvement model.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard CRUD operation on configuration data and does not drive changes in the high-level system architecture.

requirement: The user shall be able to modify the technical parameters governing the simulation, including the parameters of the improvement model and the simulation rules.
ASR_Explanation: It mandates support for dynamic configuration of simulation modules and parameter management interfaces.

requirement: The user shall be able to select the structural elements to be included in the simulation.
ASR_Explanation: This is a UI‐level functional feature with minimal impact on high‐level architectural decisions.

requirement: The user shall be able to dynamically determine bridge subsets as part of the scenario settings.
ASR_Explanation: This is a domain-level functional detail for scenario configuration and does not drive major architectural structure or cross-cutting concerns.

requirement: The system shall be capable of running a program simulation for a selected set of structures and storing the simulation results in the Pontis database.
ASR_Explanation: This drives the architectural design of the simulation engine and its integration with the Pontis database for result storage.

requirement: The user shall be able to choose from Pontis-generated work recommendations, inspector work candidates, and user-defined work items to include in the bridge simulation for a structure.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard UI selection feature with routine data integration and does not impose significant architectural constraints.

requirement: The system shall be capable of running bridge analysis for a single structure and storing the simulation results in the Pontis database.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the integration of a bridge analysis engine with the Pontis database, shaping core components, data flows, and persistence layers.

requirement: The system shall provide a Bridge Analysis Dashboard that will allow the user to choose work items for a structure and the timing of the work, view the impact of the work on the condition of the structure, assign the work candidates to a project, and perform life-cycle cost analysis for the structure. The exact functionality of the dashboard will be informed by review of the results of NCHRP Project 12-67, Multiple-Objective Optimization for Bridge Management Systems.
ASR_Explanation: This dashboard’s complex data integration, optimization algorithms and lifecycle cost analysis requirements will drive the design of system modules, data pipelines, and UI architecture.

requirement: The user shall be able to create new programs by manually entering program information.
ASR_Explanation: This is a routine CRUD operation with minimal influence on overall architecture.

requirement: The user shall be able to edit or delete existing programs.
ASR_Explanation: Standard CRUD functionality with minimal impact on overall system architecture.

requirement: The user shall be able to create new projects by manually entering the project information.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard CRUD operation with minimal impact on high-level architectural decisions.

requirement: The user shall be able to edit or delete existing projects.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard CRUD operation with minimal impact on the overall system architecture.

requirement: The user shall be able assign Pontis-generated work recommendations, inspector work candidates, and user-specified work items to a project, or remove work items from a project.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard CRUD operation on project–work item associations and does not drive any new architectural patterns.

requirement: The user shall be able to split a project into multiple projects and combine multiple projects into a master project.
ASR_Explanation: Line 2: It affects the data model and service boundaries for composite project management, requiring support for project hierarchies, merge/split operations, and consistency guarantees.

requirement: The user shall be able to edit the characteristics of multiple projects simultaneously (batch update).
ASR_Explanation: Enables bulk editing which impacts service/API design, transaction management, concurrency control, and performance considerations.

requirement: The user shall be able to edit the data stored in the Project Planning tables through the GUI.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard GUI CRUD feature with minimal impact on the overall system architecture.

requirement: The system shall be capable of tracking and linking funding levels and project budgets. The system shall warn users if the project budget exceeds funding.
ASR_Explanation: Standard business logic for data validation and user notification that can be implemented within the existing architecture.

requirement: The system shall be capable of grouping work candidates by different action types – bridge-level, element-level, and flexible-level actions.
ASR_Explanation: This is a straightforward functional requirement for data grouping and does not drive major architectural decisions.

requirement: The system shall be capable of performing single-field and cross-field validation on NBI data items in batch mode.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement impacts the design of the data-processing pipeline by introducing batch-mode and cross-field validation modules that affect scalability and performance.

requirement: The system shall be capable of performing configurable input range checks using the DATADICT table upon entering data.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the design of a dynamic, data‐driven validation framework and corresponding database schema for input checks.

requirement: The system shall be capable of performing data validation tasks including cross field validation upon saving data.
ASR_Explanation: This validation requirement is an implementation detail in the business logic layer and does not drive high-level architectural decisions.

requirement: The system shall allow users to configure data validation rules. This shall be a privileged operation.
ASR_Explanation: This requires adding a dynamic rule‐engine component with privileged access controls and persistent configuration management, impacting the overall system architecture.

requirement: The system shall provide data review wizard for comparing incoming bridge data with existing data. The errors will be highlighted for easy review.
ASR_Explanation: This drives the design of data ingestion and comparison pipelines, error-highlighting logic, and UI components for the review workflow.

requirement: The system shall guarantee atomic database transactions to ensure maximum data integrity, using standard .NET transaction management capabilities.
ASR_Explanation: It mandates the core transaction management approach, impacting data layer design, concurrency controls, and integration with .NET transaction services.

requirement: The system shall maximize multi-user concurrency and conflict resolution in the application using standard .NET session and transaction management capabilities.
ASR_Explanation: This drives key architecture decisions around session management, transaction isolation levels, and concurrency control patterns.

requirement: The user shall be able to exchange data in NBI format.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement dictates the data serialization and integration layer design by necessitating support for the NBI format in external interfaces.

requirement: The user shall be able to exchange data in PDI format as specified in the Pontis Technical Manual.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement impacts the integration and data layer by mandating a PDI format parser/exporter per the external Pontis specification.

requirement: The user shall be able to exchange data in XML format. The emerging TransXML schema specification shall be utilized once established and accepted.
ASR_Explanation: It dictates the system’s integration and data‐exchange architecture by mandating XML formatting and a specific schema.

requirement: The user shall be able to exchange bridge inspection data in PDI format.
ASR_Explanation: It mandates designing and integrating a PDI-specific data import/export interface, impacting the system’s data and integration architecture.

requirement: The user shall be able to designate a recipient for checked-out bridges.
ASR_Explanation: This is a routine UI/business‐logic feature with limited scope and no broad architectural impact.

requirement: The system shall store all export/import control information within the database for central administration, eliminating any dependency on workstation control files to manage export/import behavior.
ASR_Explanation: Centralizing export/import control in the database affects deployment architecture, database schema design, and client configuration management.

requirement: The system shall strictly separate transient or volatile batch operation control records – e.g. bridge records to be included in a report – from more static control record – e.g. currently checked-out bridges.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the data storage and module separation strategy by mandating distinct handling layers for volatile versus static control records.

requirement: The user shall be able to archive data for existing and removed structures.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement impacts the storage architecture and data‐lifecycle management by introducing archival, retention, and retrieval subsystems.

requirement: A selection/filter capability shall be provided to restrict operations on active, inactive, or all structures during any data archiving activity.
ASR_Explanation: This is a routine filtering feature for the data-archiving module and does not drive high-level architectural changes.

requirement: The system shall provide reporting mechanism for viewing archived data.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement affects the data storage and retrieval architecture by introducing an archive reporting mechanism that impacts database schema, indexing, and performance considerations.

requirement: The user shall be able to restore the archived data.
ASR_Explanation: It requires defining the storage lifecycle and backup/recovery mechanisms to support reliable archived-data restoration.

requirement: The system shall use timestamps in the database to log when rows were last changed.
ASR_Explanation: This is a straightforward database schema detail for audit logging and does not drive significant architectural structure.

requirement: The system shall continue to provide existing application integration capabilities with BRIDGEWare products.
ASR_Explanation: This is a maintenance requirement to preserve existing integration behavior and does not drive new architectural decisions.

requirement: The system shall support concurrent storage in the database of planned, active, and inactive structures, and potentially other status/lifecycle phase indicators, to facilitate BRIDGEWare integration.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the database schema, concurrency control, and integration layer design for managing multiple lifecycle status values.

requirement: The administrator shall be able to create and edit new application roles.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard role‐management feature that can be implemented within the existing security and data layers without altering the overall architecture.

requirement: The administrator shall be able to assign users to application roles.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the security architecture by requiring a role‐based access control framework and identity management integration.

requirement: The system shall provide user interface for managing application roles.
ASR_Explanation: This is a presentation‐layer feature that can be implemented within the existing architecture without major structural changes.

requirement: The administrator shall be able to assign application permissions to each user roles.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design of the security and authorization architecture by mandating a role‐based access control system.

requirement: The administrator shall be able to add new users to the system.
ASR_Explanation: This is a routine CRUD operation for user management and does not impose significant architectural constraints.

requirement: The user shall be able to modify his or her basic account profile.
ASR_Explanation: This is a routine CRUD operation on user profiles that does not drive any major architectural decisions.

requirement: The administrator shall be able to create bridge-level access filters.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the data‐plane and control‐plane design by adding bridge‐level filtering logic, affecting performance, configuration management, and module interfaces.

requirement: The administrator shall be able to assign bridge-level filters for each user.
ASR_Explanation: It necessitates adding a per-user filtering component at the bridge layer, impacting the core message processing architecture.

requirement: The administrator shall be able to remove users from the system or disable access as appropriate.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement impacts the design of the authentication/authorization subsystem, user data lifecycle management, and audit/logging architecture.

requirement: The system shall be capable of validating user name and password during log-in process.
ASR_Explanation: Standard user/password validation is a common feature and does not significantly constrain the overall system architecture.

requirement: The system shall be capable of authorizing access to Pontis functionalities depending on the user’s privileges during log-in process.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design of a security/authorization module and affects component boundaries, data flows, and integration with authentication services.

requirement: The system shall be capable of tracking login and logout activity.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design of a cross-cutting audit/logging subsystem and impacts data storage, security, and compliance considerations.

requirement: The system shall be capable of running concurrent sessions for a user.
ASR_Explanation: Line 2: Requires changes to the session‐management and concurrency architecture to ensure scalable, thread‐safe handling of multiple user sessions.

requirement: The system shall be capable of supporting Windows Authentication mechanism for database access.
ASR_Explanation: This impacts the security and deployment architecture by requiring Windows domain integration and specific database connectivity configurations.

requirement: The system shall be capable of initiating a session without a login prompt. This capability will provide support for an independent launch shell for Pontis and will be configured by the system administrator.
ASR_Explanation: Alters the authentication and security architecture by changing the login flow and integrating an independent launch shell.

requirement: The system shall be capable of integrating authentication and permissions with BRIDGEWare.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the security and integration architecture by requiring authentication protocols, permission mapping, and an external BRIDGEWare interface.

requirement: The system shall be capable of operating with a single-sign-on authentication mechanism based on Active Directory Services or the LDAP standard.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement impacts the overall security and integration architecture by dictating support for specific authentication protocols and directory services.

requirement: The administrator shall be able to configure the system units of measure in either English or metric mode. This will be a permanent setting for the application and database.
ASR_Explanation: This preference has minimal architectural impact, as it’s a standard configuration setting managed at the application and database layers.

requirement: The system shall be capable of storing the INI file configuration information in the database or workstation registry as appropriate, eliminating dependency on INI files for program operation.
ASR_Explanation: This impacts the configuration management architecture by requiring DB/registry persistence integration and altering deployment and security considerations.

requirement: The system will accommodate XP directory security and file permissions when storing any configuration or session parameters.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design of the storage and security subsystems by mandating OS‐level access control and permissions integration.

requirement: The administrator shall be able to edit the DATADICT table or its successor(s) through a GUI window.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard CRUD GUI feature for database edits and doesn’t drive any new architectural patterns or infrastructure changes.

requirement: The administrator shall be able to add and configure pre-populated new structure templates.
ASR_Explanation: This is a feature-level administrative function and does not impose significant architectural constraints.

requirement: The administrator shall be able to configure field-level security.
ASR_Explanation: It drives the design of a fine-grained access control framework at the data-model, service-layer, and UI levels, affecting overall security architecture.

requirement: The administrator shall be able to configure some fields as mandatory for user input.
ASR_Explanation: This is a form‐level configuration detail and does not impose any system-wide architectural constraints.

requirement: The administrator shall be able to configure different levels of permission to control editing of checked-out bridges.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the architecture to include a flexible role‐based access control module for dynamic permission management.

requirement: The system shall adhere to a .NET logging standard.
ASR_Explanation: It imposes a cross-cutting concern requiring integration of a .NET logging framework and related middleware/configuration across the system.

requirement: The system shall adhere to a .NET exception handling standard.
ASR_Explanation: It dictates a cross-cutting exception‐handling strategy in the .NET stack, shaping middleware, logging integration, and module boundaries.

requirement: The administrator shall be able to configure the values in the dropdown lists in the system.
ASR_Explanation: This is a routine admin CRUD feature and does not impose significant architectural constraints beyond standard configuration management.

requirement: The administrator shall be able to create agency-specific elements, including element definitions, condition states, actions, models and other related information.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard CRUD capability for administrators and does not impose significant architectural constraints.

requirement: The administrator shall be able to configure definitions of element types, environments, materials, and categories.
ASR_Explanation: It’s a standard CRUD configuration feature with minimal impact, handled by existing admin/data management components.

requirement: The administrator shall be able to add custom structure lists.
ASR_Explanation: This is a routine CRUD feature for the admin UI/business logic and does not impact the overall system architecture.

requirement: The administrator shall be able to add custom project lists.
ASR_Explanation: This is a straightforward CRUD feature for project lists with minimal impact on the overall system architecture.

requirement: The administrator shall be able to configure default values for various scenario parameters.
ASR_Explanation: This is a standard configurability feature requiring UI and data storage but without major architectural impact.

requirement: The administrator shall be able to configure customizable options that affect system operations.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design of a flexible configuration management framework and impacts system modularity for runtime adaptability.

requirement: The system shall validate all configuration options to verify proper program operation, including explicit identification of any potentially conflicting program options.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement mandates a central configuration validation and conflict‐resolution component, affecting module decomposition, dependency management, and error‐handling architecture.

requirement: Configuration option validation shall be supported by a built-in diagnostic report.
ASR_Explanation: This requirement drives the design of a built-in diagnostic/reporting subsystem and its integration with the configuration management layer.

requirement: The administrator shall be able to configure the labels of user interface items and screen displays.
ASR_Explanation: This is a UI implementation detail requiring configurable labels but does not impact the overall system architecture.

requirement: The system shall be capable of supporting internationalization using the standard Windows/.NET internationalization support.
ASR_Explanation: It drives architectural decisions around resource management, UI layering, and integration of .NET globalization/localization frameworks.

requirement: The system shall be capable of showing all screens, menus, user interface controls, prompts, error messages and information notices in native language, with some of these elements customizable.
ASR_Explanation: This introduces internationalization/localization as a cross-cutting concern requiring resource externalization, configurable UI frameworks, and translation management.

requirement: The system shall provide an interface to communicate with an external service or third-party software. A set of standard interfaces will be defined for this capability.
ASR_Explanation: It introduces an external integration boundary requiring architectural decisions on interfaces, protocols, and extensibility.

requirement: The system shall provide application administrators with a secured tool for overriding bridge/project check-out status.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the system’s security and authorization architecture by introducing privileged admin override functionality requiring secure access controls.

requirement: The system shall provide application administrators with a secured tool for clearing batch processing tables used by the system for deadlock/lockout resolution.
ASR_Explanation: It impacts the security and transactional architecture by requiring a privileged admin interface for DB‐level deadlock/lockout resolution.

requirement: The system shall provide a fixed set of predefined, secured, administration reports to document users, user roles, application privileges, user login sessions, checked-out bridges, and other key information necessary to manage application operation.
ASR_Explanation: It mandates a secure reporting module with data aggregation and storage design for admin/audit information, affecting overall system architecture.

requirement: The EIM&D SS design will be informed by World Wide Web Consortium (W3C) design guidelines and conform to common industry practices for user interaction behavior and user interface design. For the thin-client portion of Pontis, the AASHTO standards for thin-client web-based applications software will be followed.
ASR_Explanation: Mandating W3C and AASHTO compliance drives front-end architectural decisions, framework selection, and integration patterns.

requirement: The software, through its user interface, will clearly identify the product name (Pontis 5.0.x), the licensee, the connected user, and the organization that developed the system (AASHTO). Additional session identification information will be available on demand.
ASR_Explanation: This is a cosmetic UI labeling requirement with minimal impact on overall system structure or quality attributes.
